programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: DI Morell investigates Honey's apparent abduction by new husband Scott, little suspecting she has fled with him of her own volition and is preparing to set sail for France. Unfortunately, he blurts out a confession of his guilt as they're waiting for the boat to arrive, leaving her facing the uncomfortable realisation that she's trapped with a murderer. Back at Sun Hill, Yvonne leaves herself open to charges of using excessive force to detain a suspect and Amber teases Dan Casper about his alleged run-in with a gang of armed youths.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Honey is interviewed by Adam and fights to save her career, insisting she was abducted by new husband Scott. JT begins to suspect Gina has cancer, and Dan alleges Amber has been blackmailing him to do menial duties on her behalf, while Yvonne is devastated to learn Lee Thomas, the young thug she forcibly restrained at the cemetery, has died of his injuries.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of three. Yvonne is suspended from duty pending further investigation into the death of young thug Lee Thomas, and Leela is also accused of assaulting a suspect, leaving the relief uncertain as to what constitutes reasonable force when making an arrest. Fearful of leaving himself open to accusations of heavy-handedness or police brutality, Tony later hesitates to use force in a confrontation with local youths - and suffers a horrific beating. Meanwhile Zain Nadir agrees to do a stint in CID before beginning his next undercover job.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of three. As Tony recovers in hospital, Gabriel and Roger are disciplined for using their CS spray in a tense situation, prompting June to stand up for coppers' rights and call the demoralised relief out on strike. Unfortunately for Lance, he's already been dragged out on an unauthorised mission by Zain Nadir, and ends up being held at gunpoint by a local gangster. On the personal front, Phil receives the shocking news that Kate Maltby has been rushed to hospital and might lose their baby, while Dan receives a letter in the internal mail from someone who clearly knows about his affair with Sun Hill newcomer Rochelle Barratt.(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A young nun is found dead in a smoke-filled bedroom in what seems like a terrible accident caused by a lit cigarette. But when Richard deduces it must have been murder, there are no shortage of suspects, including other nuns, a priest and the mother superior - as well as an unscrupulous PR man promoting a holy spring in the convent grounds. The question is, with the room locked from the inside, how could anyone have committed the crime in the first place? Kenneth Cranham, Patrick Baladi and Caroline Langrishe guest star.(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A body is discovered floating in the pool of an exclusive cosmetic surgery clinic and several things point to suicide - the victim was going through a messy divorce and there is an empty pill box in her room. But DI Poole has several reasons to believe Valerie Dupree was murdered - not least the fact she poured herself a cup of the world's most expensive tea but only drank half. The pressure is on to find the culprit as the clinic is highly respected on the island and the chief surgeon is an old friend of Commissioner Patterson - and as the detectives begin to dig, they discover many sordid secrets among the guests and staff. Cherie Lunghi (Secret Diary of a Call Girl), James Fleet (The Vicar of Dibley) and Emma Pierson (Hotel Babylon) guest star.(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Treasure hunters arrive on the island to track down the loot of a legendary pirate, a search that comes to an abrupt halt when the party's geologist is shot dead in leader Daniel Morgan's tent. As the murder weapon was a 300-year-old pistol, DI Poole decides every member of the team is a suspect - but the investigation takes a strange turn when Daniel falls victim to a series of unfortunate and coincidental accidents. Has a mythical pirate's curse become a reality or is someone from the 21st century simply trying to bump him off? Ben Miller and Sara Martins star, with Michael Brandon (Dempsey and Makepeace) and Kelly Adams (Hustle).(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Dr Turner and Shelagh are at the forefront of the advances in medical technology, and begin a campaign to ensure better respiratory health for their patients. However, Timothy fears their own lifestyle is not good for them and tries to encourage his parents to give up smoking. Violet injures her back and is forced to leave the shop in Fred's hands, a decision that strengthens their relationship but which proves to have other, unforeseen consequences. A new mother's past comes to light, with devastating repercussions for her family. Nurse Crane draws on her own experiences to help her.(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Poplar is rocked by a series of violent attacks on women, and a prostitute is too ashamed to report what she knows - but her actions lead to a dangerous man roaming the streets, with devastating consequences for the residents of Nonnatus House. A mother tries to keep her daughter's pregnancy a secret to avoid gossip, putting her life in danger. The Turners go on a long overdue holiday, but the weather ruins their plans.(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The introduction of the contraceptive pill is welcomed by Dr Turner, but some of his colleagues are less keen on the idea. Patsy tries to help a bargee who wants to give birth among her own community rather than in a maternity ward. Nurse Crane deals with an emergency during a storm, and a familiar face returns to Nonnatus House - but has been changed by her time away.(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Dr Turner is horrified to realise he may be partly to blame as the cause of the recent rise in birth deformities comes to light, and tries to prevent further cases. Sister Evangelina returns to work, but has clearly been affected badly by her stroke, and her health continues to deteriorate. A pregnant bride goes into labour during her wedding reception, while her mother is forced to come to terms with her grandchild being conceived out of wedlock.(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Christmas special of the period drama, which catches up with the nurses and midwives of Nonnatus House in 1962. They receive a letter from a doctor running a mission clinic on the Eastern Cape of South Africa, who is in desperate need of their help to save her urgently needed hospital from closure. The nurses head to Africa to lend a hand, finding the clinic understaffed, underfunded and with a poor water supply, but overwhelmed with patients who need their help more than anyone ever has before. Sinead Cusack guest stars.(n)

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: Kristin, Breen and Gina witness a fatal skydiving accident while celebrating Mike's birthday with a picnic. To make matters worse, Kristin knows the victim - entrepreneur Andre Barrington, an ex-boyfriend. When the cords are found severed, it becomes clear that his demise was either suicide or murder.(n)

programme.title: Lady Chatterley
programme.desc: Connie's marriage begins to fall apart, so she flees to her father's seaside retreat. But before she leaves, she must fulfil her promise to Mellors of one last night together. Starring Joely Richardson and Sean Bean.(n)

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: As a third victim is found murdered, Marilyn soon identifies the man responsible as Damon Morton (Iain Glen), and Walker must gather evidence to secure his conviction.(n)

programme.title: Garden Hopping
programme.desc: Daphne Ledward visits the Headland Garden in Polruan, Cornwall - one of the participants opening its havens to the public in the National Gardens Scheme - where plants face the harsh conditions of salt air and high winds.(n)

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: The CO has a lot on his mind after a civilian gets caught in the crossfire during a training exercise, causing an embittered Mark to fly off the handle. Julie has a surprise for Tony and Jacko, but ends up learning a harsh lesson, while Karen's son Kevin wonders if he might be gay.(n)

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: Fitzpatrick's domestic life is thrown into turmoil by a stunning revelation from Jacko, and Andy ends up bearing the brunt of Mark's traumatic experiences of civilian life.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part three of three. Adam tries to defuse the threat of strike action by the relief, reminding them of their duties as police officers and offering to let Yvonne return to the station while the inquiry takes its course. Dan reacts with dismay when Rochelle suggests they put their affair on hold, Gary chafes under Suzie's condescending attitude, and Samantha looks on in disbelief as the simmering hostility between brothers Phil and Steve erupts into violence.(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: A woman comes in for a heart transplant, and Connie persuades her to accept an experimental procedure that has never been tried before. Ric leaves Nick in charge while he is at the university, but Jac does not approve of his handling of a cancer patient's treatment and a past conflict between them resurfaces.(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Josh regrets a moment of kindness toward an unprofessional colleague caught moonlighting as a taxi driver to earn some extra cash, and Sunny faces the music at his disciplinary hearing. Hospital chaplain Peter offers spiritual support to Eve, then stares tragedy in the face himself. Derek Thompson and Ian Bleasdale star.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.sub-title: One Step Too Far - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. Laura looks at Gabriel with new eyes when he takes a surprisingly compassionate approach to her wayward son Liam, who is being bullied because of her job. Adam tries to mend fences with the rank-and-file officers by going out on the beat with June - only to lose his cool completely when a traffic accident triggers painful memories. Samantha jeopardises Jo's investigation by poaching her informant, leading to a bitter row, while Gary's future on the force looks uncertain after he reacts to Suzie's constant needling with a very public outburst.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Grant meets up with an old mate from the army. Hattie and Steve are bickering and Clyde would like Gidea to stay with him in Walford.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Sharon is reluctant to tell Phil that her relationship with Grant is back on.(n)

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: Nick travels to Cyprus in an effort to locate Ariadne and bring her home, but things do not turn out as planned. Meanwhile, Sally visits her sick father.(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Camille's best friend, a singer on a Caribbean party boat, collapses on stage during a night of music and cocktails. After she dies in the policewoman's arms, the officers question the vessel's staff and on-board tourists in an effort to get to the bottom of her mysterious demise. Photographs taken on the evening offer a fuller picture of what took place, but could the answer lie in the vocalist's pre-show routine? Meanwhile, DI Poole tries his best to comfort a grieving Camille, but it soon becomes clear that sensitivity is not his strong suit. Ben Miller and Sara Martins star, with guest appearances from singer Jamelia, Dexter Fletcher (Lock, Stock and Two Smoking Barrels) and Amanda Mealing (ex-Holby City).(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: An English tourist is strangled in her villa by a killer using the victim's scarf. The crime scene indicates a robbery gone wrong, but DI Poole recalls an almost identical case back in London. He grows even more suspicious when the victim's husband turns out to be crooked former colleague Doug Anderson, a name he had hoped was consigned to the past. But even though he has a watertight alibi and the team move on to further leads, Poole can't shake the notion that his old adversary is somehow involved. Ben Miller stars, with guest appearances from Neil Pearson, Ralf Little and Miller's former Primeval co-stars Hannah Spearritt and James Murray.(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo is invited to lay a wreath at Dunkirk for the millennium - but gets into trouble due to his dress sense. Christmas special from 1999, starring Bill Owen in the last performance he filmed before his death.(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: Who's That Dancing with Nora Batty, Then?
programme.desc: Clegg, Compo and Blamire look for a piano to provide the entertainment at Gloria's surprise farewell party before she emigrates to Australia. Meanwhile, two new librarians arrive and are bent on keeping the morality of the facility in tact.(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The islanders batten down the hatches as a hurricane approaches Saint Marie - but the police can't take cover as they're called to another murder, this time at the local university. The victim is a meteorologist, whose death has been staged to look like an accident - and the plot thickens when it emerges he had something important to tell the dean but died before he got the chance. To confuse matters, all his colleagues seem to have a reason for wanting him dead. Can the team unmask the culprit before the storm hits? Ben Miller and Sara Martins star, with Gavin & Stacey actor Mathew Horne.(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: When a shot is fired at a charity fundraiser, host Malcolm Powell is found dead in his study. The victim's PA is certain she knows who the culprit is - a mystery man named Jack Roberts who had an appointment with her boss and was seen fleeing from the scene of the crime. Richard and his team find the suspect's abandoned car nearby with no clues as to his whereabouts or motive for the killing. However, a background check on the dead man reveals that he and his wife defrauded investors out of millions of pounds back in England. Guest starring Sean Pertwee, Lucy Davis and Julie Graham.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The former leader of an Irish Republican group comes forward with information regarding the abduction and subsequent disappearance of an 18-year-old woman in 1983, prompting the team to reopen the case. The reformed dissident claims the rebels used the kidnapping to raise their profile, but his subsequent reinvention as a politician and man of peace leaves Sandra and her colleagues suspicious of his motives. They also investigate whether the arms-manufacturing activities of the victim's father could have made her a target. Crime drama, guest starring Sian Phillips and Annette Crosbie, with Amanda Redman and James Bolam.(n)

programme.title: Lady Chatterley
programme.desc: Mellors' wife returns, and begins spreading rumours of her husband's relationship with Lady Chatterley. However, when news reaches Sir Clifford, Connie responds by spinning a tale of her own. Last in the series, starring Joely Richardson and Sean Bean.(n)

programme.sub-title: One Step Too Far - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. Laura looks at Gabriel with new eyes when he takes a surprisingly compassionate approach to her wayward son Liam, who is being bullied because of her job. programme.desc: Daphne Ledward visits the Winsford Walled Garden in Devon, to answer the owner's horticultural queries.(n)

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: Tony tries to help Jacko forget Julie by taking him on a double date, while Jessica exposes corruption in high places. The McCleods hear the patter of tiny feet and recent events finally take their toll on Mark, who makes an inspection of the barracks go with something of a bang.(n)

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: Drysdale decides to send the boys on an escort mission to Africa, but it proves to be a fiasco on every level - particularly for Jeremy, who first refuses to follow the CO's orders to the letter, then has a bitter reunion with ex-wife Lilian that sets the sparks flying. Mark also resurfaces, as a mercenary soldier fighting for the enemy and keen to gain revenge on his former King's Own colleagues.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.sub-title: One Step Too Far - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. Laura looks at Gabriel with new eyes when he takes a surprisingly compassionate approach to her wayward son Liam, who is being bullied because of her job. Adam tries to mend fences with the rank-and-file officers by going out on the beat with June - only to lose his cool completely when a traffic accident triggers painful memories. Samantha jeopardises Jo's investigation by poaching her informant, leading to a bitter row, while Gary's future on the force looks uncertain after he reacts to Suzie's constant needling with a very public outburst.(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Diane is assigned to work with Nick, and when they find a cocaine package inside a patient, the duo end up lying to Ric and the police. Sam tries to set up Joseph with a new nurse, but he is not interested. Ryan resurfaces, and Mark tries to persuade him to turn himself in, but the terrified teenager lashes out and stabs Matt. Patsy Byrne guest stars.(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: The staff get into the spirit of Halloween and Adam begins his first shift as a nurse in A&E. A sombre Eve, shaken after treating a drug overdose patient, makes a life-changing decision. Chloe gets into another argument with Tina - and reveals she is having an abortion the very next day. Hospital drama, starring Cathy Shipton, Barbara Marten, Claire Goose and Rebecca Wheatley.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.sub-title: One Step Too Far - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. Laura begins to feel increasingly unfulfilled in her role as PCSO, and announces to Smithy that she wants to become a PC. Gina's old flame Jonathan Fox returns to Sun Hill, and Neil pressures Gary to drop his bullying accusations against Susie Sim. Gabriel warns an increasingly infatuated Sheelagh not to get involved with him, while Samantha continues to needle Jo, fuelling an already volatile situation.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: While attending a conference, David receives a job offer that seems impossible to refuse _ will he leave Simon in the lurch? Karen anxiously prepares to meet her estranged mother for lunch, and Chris learns the truth about Beth's marital status. Anna treats a man whose health is suffering due to his job in a textile factory, but ends up equally concerned about his wife's welfare as she struggles to look after her husband and two lazy teenagers. Drama, starring Leslie Ash and Philip Middlemiss.(n)

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: Jo Mills is persuaded to act on behalf of a drug addict, who went on the run 16 years previously after being charged with attempted murder. The defendant alleges detectives forced him to sign a confession and allowed him to escape custody - leaving Deed to decide if they should be charged with perverting the course of justice. Starring Martin Shaw, Donald Sinden, Jenny Seagrove, Caroline Langrishe and Robert Cavanah.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The gun used to kill a boxer is discovered at the scene of an armed robbery 15 years later, prompting the team to reinvestigate the case. Twenty-one-year-old Eddie Mayfair was shot dead a week after winning an important bout, which left his opponent seriously injured and unable to carry on his career. The detectives initially suspect revenge as a motive, but the case is complicated when they uncover the rivalries and money-making aspects of the sport. Guest starring Maggie O'Neill (Shameless) and Chloe Howman (Casualty).(n)

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The discovery of human remains in an urban canal is linked to the unsolved murder of a prison governor 15 years earlier, leading the team to seek out the original suspects. However, it is not long before the prison psychologist, who is married to the victim's daughter, finds himself personally embroiled in the investigation. Meanwhile, Boyd launches a search for his son Luke.(n)

programme.desc: Fifty years on, David Attenborough returns to Madagascar to see how the island has changed since his first visit, and to search for more clues about one of his most treasured possessions, the egg of an elephant bird, an ostrich-like creature that weighed half a ton. Scientists perform tests to determine the age of the egg, as the naturalist discovers whether the bird's extinction can shed light on what is happening on the island today.(n)

Essie faces her first round of chemotherapy, and Sacha begs her to tell her family and friends the truth. Nicky is given the opportunity to carry out a challenging heart transplant under Jac's supervision.(n)

programme.title: Our Girl
programme.desc: The soldiers on the rescue mission make it safely back to the hospital, only to be confronted with an unexpected situation that leaves the whole section reeling. Georgie returns to the UK, trying to conceal her trauma, but is horrified to discover that Omar has struck a deal with the US to escape justice in return for providing information. When she angrily challenges the brigadier over this, she finds herself facing a mental health evaluation that leaves her future in the army hanging in the balance.(n)

programme.title: Hot Property
programme.desc: Yung Filly helps a Birmingham woman explore three potential partners' bedrooms as she searches for a confident man who shares her passion for music - but uncovers everything from balaclavas to spiders in the bed.(n)

programme.title: Killing Eve
programme.desc: Villanelle takes to her new vocation as a spy, even if she does not care for Eve's methods. She dives deep into the dangerous world of her target under the guise of her newfound persona, Billie, but finds that a new breakthrough comes with strings attached. Meanwhile, Eve may have competition for Villanelle's affections. Spy thriller, starring Jodie Comer and Sandra Oh.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As the days get longer and warmer, the Gardeners' World team returns to guide enthusiasts through seasonal highlights from across the country visiting stunning gardens, meeting the gardeners and finding out their secrets of success. This week, Adam Frost heads into his garden to offer inspiration for viewers' own patches along with all the essential gardening jobs for the coming weekend.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: It is all systems go in the lambing shed as Adam Henson and Helen Skelton work round the clock to help a Wirral farmer at one of his busiest times of the year. Plus, Sean Fletcher is in Carmarthenshire to learn first-hand about the rigours of lambing outdoors.(n)

programme.title: Heavenly Gardens with Alexander Armstrong
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which the presenter and garden designer Arit Anderson visit gardens of reflection and contemplation. At the remote Pluscarden Abbey in north-east Scotland, Alexander helps harvest luscious apples and accompanies the monks singing midday psalms. Meanwhile, Arit walks in the footsteps of royalty in the glorious grounds of Sudeley Castle. Plus, Alexander returns to his student haunts in Cambridge to discover a place designed to inspire joy, even on the bleakest days, in the university's Botanic Garden.(n)

programme.title: The Bear Family and Me
programme.desc: Gordon Buchanan returns to Minnesota at the beginning of autumn to check how abandoned bear cub Hope is progressing and is delighted to discover that her life has taken a turn for the better. However, a fresh danger is posed by the arrival of hunters in the forest and Gordon decides to walk with the animals to protect them.(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Professional chefs compete for a place at a banquet celebrating children's literature. Here, the two highest-scoring chefs from Northern Ireland cook their six-course menus again for Matthew Fort, Oliver Peyton and Andi Oliver, who are joined by guest Cerrie Burnell, who writes children's books with an emphasis on diversity and inclusivity. Presented by Susan Calman.(n)

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Simple Cooking
programme.desc: The food writer explores unusual ingredients and quirky twists on classic recipes, including a black-pudding sausage roll, a bread-and-butter dessert made from caramelised bananas, and a savoury apple crumble. In Derbyshire, Nigel also samples farmer Tom Clarke's signature boar burger.(n)

programme.title: Mexico: Earth's Festival of Life
programme.desc: The series concludes by exploring the north of the country, which is dominated by two deserts - the Sonoran and the Chihuahuan. The programme explores the conditions that have shaped this environment, and reveals how animals including prairie dogs, pygmy owls and aplomado falcons have adapted to thrive in these arid conditions.(n)

programme.title: Hugh's Wild West
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall's West Country tour continues as he visits the Somerset Levels, where he witnesses the natural spectacle of huge flocks of starlings swirling in the sky as they come in to roost during the winter. He also discovers how the Levels' dedicated volunteers have helped to maintain crucial reedbeds, which have now become havens for rare bitterns and great white egrets. Plus, Hugh observes the large blue butterfly, which has been brought back to the region from near-extinction, and meets nature enthusiast Stephen Hembury, who has been capturing local wildlife on film, but is having little luck recording elusive hares.(n)

programme.title: Death on the Nile (1978)
programme.desc: Detective Hercule Poirot investigates a murder on board a paddle steamer in Egypt. The victim was an unpopular heiress, whose outspoken manner put her at odds with most of her fellow passengers during their cruise on the Nile. Agatha Christie mystery, starring Peter Ustinov, Bette Davis, Maggie Smith, George Kennedy, Mia Farrow, Jane Birkin, Olivia Hussey, Angela Lansbury and David Niven.(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A retrospective look at TV appearances made over the years by award-winning actor Albert Finney, with interviews from the archive and classic clips capturing the milestones and highlights of his life and career. Narrated by Sylvia Syms.(n)

programme.title: The Masters 2019: Tiger Roars Again
programme.desc: A look back at Tiger Woods' memorable victory at Augusta National Golf Club, which saw the American win this tournament for the fifth time in his career, and raise his overall tally of Major victories to 15. Woods is second only to Jack Nicklaus, who triumphed six times at this famous golf course, and finished with 18 Major titles to his name.(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a bakery that produces 180,000 Cornish pasties a day, and discovers the very specific rule one of the pastries must comply with to genuinely be considered Cornish. Ruth Goodman debunks some myths about pasties and investigates the history of the pepper trade, while Cherry Healey learns about the science of onions - including why cutting them can bring tears to the eyes.(n)

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The explorer travels from Texas to southern Mexico, meeting a woman who risks her life to take supplies to migrants stranded on the Mexican side of the border in Reynosa, one of the country's most violent cities, which is dominated by a powerful criminal organisation. He also ventures into the rainforest to explore the Maya city of Yaxchilan, and meets the modern-day descendants of this civilisation, who are increasingly marginalised and treated as second-class citizens.(n)

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The teams prepare to leave the checkpoint in Cafayate, Argentina, except for Dom and Lizzie, whose disastrous last leg has left them more than 24 hours behind their competitors. The next destination is the Brazilian island of Ilha Grande, halfway between Rio de Janeiro and Sao Paolo, and at 3,600 kilometres, it is the longest stage of the journey. Emon and Jamiul decide to travel through Paraguay, while Jen and Rob decide they need to see more of the continent, and stop off at the famous Iguazu Falls.(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Entrepreneurs pitch more money-making ideas to panellists Sara Davies, Touker Suleyman, Deborah Meaden, Tej Lalvani and Peter Jones. Two Syrian refugees share their alternative to Cypriot halloumi, a couple showcase some virus filtration gadgets, and a charitable entrepreneur has a plan for second-hand clothes. Presented by Evan Davis.(n)

programme.title: Trigonometry
programme.desc: Ray's mother visits London for her first birthday since leaving home. As she tries to make sense of the growing divide between herself and her daughter, she begins to pick up on the unusual dynamic between Ray and her two landlords during a tense birthday dinner.(n)

programme.title: Limmy's Homemade Show
programme.desc: Rough-and-ready self-made comedy show. Limmy worries about getting a real job, spots something terrifying in his mirror and gives terrible advice to DJs - advice that might result in being hit by a flying bottle.(n)

programme.title: Effie Gray (2014)
programme.desc: A Victorian woman is trapped in a loveless marriage to a celebrated art critic from the age of 19. She becomes a model for a painter her husband is promoting and falls in love with him. Inspired by the relationship and aided by a respected writer, she creates scandal by becoming the first woman to seek a divorce. Fact-based period drama, starring Dakota Fanning and Emma Thompson.(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week.(n)

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: A serial insurance fraudster tries to cash in on horrific terror attacks, and the former taxman who devised a scam to con the public out of nearly Â£7 million.(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Max is tempted to start drinking again after meeting her daughter. Although she manages to resist, she still raises suspicions among her colleagues that she has relapsed, and is accused of being unfit to perform surgery. David is determined to prove his worth, and joins Fletch in making a complaint against the nurses being moved around the hospital. Essie keeps her news from Sacha, which puts Dom in the awkward position of having to life for her.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Night Force
programme.desc: A glimpse into the lives of the invisible army of workers driving lorries, making milk deliveries and cleaning streets at night. Plus, the radio DJ who keeps the insomniacs of Newcastle company.(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace preside as six more hopefuls compete in the fifth and final week of heats. They are challenged to serve up their signature dish, with the judges' favourite two winning a coveted MasterChef apron and a place in the next round. The others must come up with a dish using lemon sole, peppers and Swiss chard, after which two cooks are sent home, leaving the remaining four to cook two courses for 2018 contestant Anthony O'Shaughnessy and former champions Steven Wallis and Shelina Permalloo.(n)

programme.title: Lambing Live
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Adam Henson revisit farming families from previous series to explore their connection to the land, before heading to Scotland to meet a few of the farmers making a living north of the border.(n)

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: The Story of Vaisakhi
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the rituals associated with Vaisakhi, the annual Sikh festival that marks the occasion on which Guru Gobind Singh established the Khalsa - the wider body of formally baptised followers. The programme provides an insight into some of the colourful parades that will take place across the UK to celebrate the day, and explains the major themes that influence the daily lives of modern adherents. Includes contributions by Sikh historians and religious experts.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Nature's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: Chris Packham presents the first of two documentaries investigating unusual natural phenomena, with the help of eyewitness accounts, scientists and footage of the occurrences. The phenomena he explores include a storm that turned Sydney crimson, moths that cocooned a car in the Netherlands, a case of exploding toads in Germany and disturbing plagues of mice and locusts.(n)

programme.title: The Robe (1953)
programme.desc: A callous Roman centurion is assigned to oversee Christ's crucifixion, an experience he finds so moving that he decides to convert to the Christian faith in a bid to redeem himself. However, his new loyalties incur the wrath of the insane Emperor Caligula. Drama based on the novel by Lloyd C Douglas, starring Richard Burton, Jean Simmons, Victor Mature and Michael Rennie.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Penrhyn Castle in north Wales, experts Adam Partridge and David Harper come across a rare first edition of Dracula and a colourful Victorian watercolour set. Meanwhile, presenter Paul Martin gets to try his hand at splitting slate while learning about one of Wales' oldest industries.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: The return after a mid-series break of the quiz in which four famous faces take part in a week of trivia-based games, beginning with Rachel Riley, JB Gill, Katie Derham and Richard Herring.(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Abandoned cheetah cub Xena is reunited with its mother and siblings after being hand-reared, while Kate Humble is in the Tiger House to find out about the death of Shouri.(n)

programme.title: Mary Berry's Easter Feast
programme.desc: Part two of two. Mary explores the foods that bring different communities together on Easter Sunday, the most symbolic and meaningful feast in the Christian calendar. As well as sharing her own family favourites, Mary discovers how the Greek Orthodox community breaks the fast with tsoureki bread, spends a day with Archbishop of York John Sentamu and discovers Filipino and Italian Easter specialities. She also puts on a feast at her local church hall for everyone she has met along the way.(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys hosts the fourth semi-final of the quiz, inviting four returning contenders to brave the black chair once more. They answer questions on the specialist subjects of the Apollo space programme, the Pitch Perfect films, the Appalachian trail, and Talking Heads, before they each have two minutes to demonstrate their general knowledge.(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The second and concluding semi-final sees two teams of four students battling it out to make it to the climax of the competition and the chance to take home the trophy. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions.(n)

programme.title: Dolly Parton: 50 Years at the Opry
programme.desc: Highlights from the singer's performance to celebrate 50 years as a member of the Grand Ole Opry, recorded in Nashville in October 2019. Featuring hits including Jolene, 9 to 5, Coat of Many Colours and I Will Always Love You, as well as appearances by Lady Antebellum, Emmylou Harris and Hank Williams Jr.(n)

programme.title: Sisters in Country: Dolly, Linda and Emmylou
programme.desc: How Dolly Parton, Linda Ronstadt and Emmylou Harris's careers took off in the 1970s with very distinct takes on country music, before they came together to collaborate on a successful album in 1987. The artists talk about uniting as harmony singers, focusing on how their alliance made them pioneers in bringing different music worlds together and raising the game for women in the country tradition.(n)

programme.title: Glastonbury 2014
programme.desc: The country singer's complete set on the Pyramid stage at Worthy Farm in the now-traditional legends spot on Sunday afternoon, featuring Jolene, Coat of Many Colours, 9 to 5, Islands in the Stream and I Will Always Love You.(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: British White cattle farmers Vivien, a retired GP, and David and Angela hope to help keep their rare breeds alive as they compete at the 104th Tendring Show in Essex. Tendring is their local show and it means a lot to them and the future of their beloved herds.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: It is all systems go in the lambing shed as Adam Henson and Helen Skelton work round the clock to help a Wirral farmer at one of his busiest times of the year. Plus, Sean Fletcher is in Carmarthenshire to learn first-hand about the rigours of lambing outdoors.(n)

programme.title: Night Force
programme.desc: Documentary following the lives of the army of night workers who toil through the small hours to get the world ready for when the rest of the population awakes.(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. Alan Reed from Warwick hopes upholstery expert Hannah Weston Smith can restore his little piece of history, a leather armchair that belonged to his grandparents, while Steve Kember and Will Kirk take a look at Polyphon music box. Elsewhere, a faded portrait with a touching history, and a shattered stained glass door panel are also restored back to their former glory by experts Lucia Scalisi and Matt Nickels.(n)

programme.title: Lambing Live
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Adam Henson are in the Scottish Borders with the Dykes as they prepare to deliver and rear a flock of newborn lambs. Kate has been learning over the past six months that having a few sheep at home is very different to managing over a thousand on the hills, while Adam shares his findings after travelling round the UK to explore the secret life of sheep.(n)

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Curiosity
programme.desc: Paul Martin hosts as Terry and Simon from Essex take on Trace and Mark from Lancashire, competing to build the most valuable collection of antiques for a chance to win Â£1,000.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Nature's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: Part two of two. Chris Packham investigates the cause behind thousands of birds falling from the sky in the south of America, resulting in panic among residents and predictions of the apocalypse. He also explores the sea foam that gives parts of the Australian coast the appearance of a bubble bath, fish falling from the sky in south London and holes that open in the Earth's crust large enough to swallow buildings, and in Florida even made a lake disappear.(n)

programme.title: The Long Ships (1963)
programme.desc: A Viking warrior steals a ship and leads his crew in search of a legendary bell, rumoured to be three times the size of a man and made of solid gold. But they have reckoned without the ambition of the king of the Moors, who captures the Vikings and intends to use them to find the prize for himself. Adventure, starring Richard Widmark, Sidney Poitier, Rosanna Schiaffino, Russ Tamblyn and Lionel Jeffries.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion and Nick Davies are at Huddersfield Town Hall to find items to take to auction, while Paul Martin finds out about former prime minister Harold Wilson.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: The host welcomes back mathematician Rachel Riley, singer and farmer JB Gill, newscaster Katie Derham and comedian Richard Herring, who all put their knowledge to the test.(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: A vet battles to save the life of a pregnant deer and its unborn calf, which is in a dangerous breach position. Jean Johansson finds out how fast a chameleon's tongue can move.(n)

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The teams prepare to leave the checkpoint in Cafayate, Argentina, except for Dom and Lizzie, whose disastrous last leg has left them more than 24 hours behind their competitors. The next destination is the Brazilian island of Ilha Grande, halfway between Rio de Janeiro and Sao Paolo, and at 3,600 kilometres, it is the longest stage of the journey. Emon and Jamiul decide to travel through Paraguay, while Jen and Rob decide they need to see more of the continent, and stop off at the famous Iguazu Falls.(n)

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Laura Jane Clark and Robert Jamison use virtual reality to redesign a 1930s Surrey house with a poorly designed layout to make better use of the space, and allow the owners to both start a family and have their parents move in with them in the future. Laura's plan involves turning the property into a light-filled interconnected series of spaces, while Robert turns conventional wisdom on its head with a visually striking and flexible design. After experiencing both potential houses, the owners must choose which they would like to put into practice.(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a foundry in France that produces iron pots and pans at the rate of one every five seconds, while Cherry Healey is in South Africa, at one of the world's largest iron ore mines, and rides in a giant dumper truck. Historian Ruth Goodman explains how cooking in pots has evolved, from communal ovens to 1970s slow cookers.(n)

programme.title: I'll Get This - Extra Helping
programme.desc: Extended version of the show in which five celebrities go out for dinner together. Jonathan Ross, Gabby Logan, Mark Wright, Anita Rani and Rachel Parris play a series of games, with the overall loser picking up the bill for the whole group.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Kirsty Wark presents analysis of the day's events.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Belsen: Our Story
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the concentration camp, featuring personal accounts from the few remaining survivors and archive footage shot by the British forces that liberated them. Bergen-Belsen was used to hold prisoners evacuated from camps that had fallen to the Allied advance, leading its population to increase to nearly 60,000 by the winter of 1944. Thousands died at the camp from starvation and disease, their bodies left unburied. The British and Canadian forces who discovered the camp were left with no choice but to burn it to the ground.(n)

programme.title: Sacred Songs - The Secrets of Our Hearts
programme.desc: British vocal ensemble Tenebrae give a special performance of music for Easter, with all 20 of its singers filmed and recorded separately, within their enforced isolation at home, under the direction of Nigel Short. The repertoire includes Bach: Wenn ich einmal so scheiden, Lobo: Versa est in luctum, Allegri: Miserere, Purcell/Croft: Thou knowest, Lord, the secrets of our hearts, Parry: My soul, there is a country and Bach: Ach Herr, lass dein lieb engelein.(n)

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming explores the English canal network, beginning with a journey from Shardlow to Etruria Junction, Stoke on Trent, exploring the Trent and Mersey Canal. However, not everything goes to plan, and he will have to deal with a tricky issue with his propeller as well as navigating the complicated locks on the Trent and Mersey Canal.(n)

programme.title: The British Garden: Life and Death on Your Lawn
programme.desc: Chris Packham and a group of experts spend 12 months scrutinising the wildlife in eight gardens on a suburban street in Welwyn Garden City. Throughout the seasons they find male crickets that bribe females with food during sex, spiders that change colour to help catch prey, and ferocious life-and-death battles in the compost heap. They also shows a different side to familiar garden residents, revealing how a robin's red breast is actually war paint, and a single litter of foxes can have up to five different fathers.(n)

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: How ESA's Solar Orbiter was built, how it will protect itself against the Sun's searing heat and how it will investigate the mysteries of the Sun. Presented by Chris Lintott and Pete Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Henry VIII is desperate to end his marriage to Katherine of Aragon, but Cardinal Wolsey's efforts to persuade the pope to grant an annulment are fruitless - much to the dismay of Thomas Cromwell, a lawyer dependent on Wolsey's patronage. The king's new mistress is rumoured to be Anne Boleyn, who has sworn vengeance on Wolsey after he refused to let her marry Harry Percy, so Cromwell visits her in the hope of swaying her to the cardinal's side. Historical drama adapted by Peter Straughan from Hilary Mantel's Booker Prize-winning novels Wolf Hall and Bring Up the Bodies, and starring Mark Rylance, Damian Lewis, Claire Foy and Jonathan Pryce.(n)

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Anne Boleyn summons Cromwell, anxious to know whether she has an ally in him, and he witnesses her venting her frustrations on her ladies-in-waiting. The king starts to take notice of Cromwell and comes to rely on the lawyer's advice, but then news arrives from York that Cardinal Wolsey has been arrested by Harry Percy, a vengeful act for denying his betrothal to Anne eight years earlier. Historical drama adapted from Hilary Mantel's Booker prize-winning novels Wolf Hall and Bring Up the Bodies, starring Mark Rylance, Damian Lewis, Claire Foy and Jonathan Pryce.(n)

programme.title: Handmade in Bolton
programme.desc: Shaun Greenhalgh is set the task of making a Renaissance animal plate of the type invented by Bernard Palissy. The 16th-century French potter used live reptiles and other animals on his ceramic ware, but for Shaun, killing grass snakes in Lancashire is not an option. So how can he source some examples for his moulds? Presented by Janina Ramirez.(n)

programme.title: Holst and Vaughan Williams: Making Music English
programme.desc: Historian Amanda Vickery and broadcaster Tom Service unearth the fascinating story of the life-long friendship between composers Ralph Vaughan Williams and Gustav Holst, whose music gave birth to the 'English sound' in the first half of the 20th century. They retrace the walking trips the two composers took together across the country to discover how influences ranging from the Renaissance masters to the folk genre imbued their music with the 'Englishness' recognised today.(n)

programme.title: Holst: The Planets with Professor Brian Cox
programme.desc: A chance to see the BBC Symphony Orchestra perform British composer Gustav Holst's The Planets at the London Barbican, in a concert from last September timed to celebrate the centenary of the premiere. But there's a special scientific twist to this concert, as before each movement, Professor Brian Cox discusses what modern science reveals about each of the planets.(n)

programme.title: Sacred Songs - The Secrets of Our Hearts
programme.desc: British vocal ensemble Tenebrae give a special performance of music for Easter, with all 20 of its singers filmed and recorded separately, within their enforced isolation at home, under the direction of Nigel Short. The repertoire includes Bach: Wenn ich einmal so scheiden, Lobo: Versa est in luctum, Allegri: Miserere, Purcell/Croft: Thou knowest, Lord, the secrets of our hearts, Parry: My soul, there is a country and Bach: Ach Herr, lass dein lieb engelein.(n)

programme.title: The Great Mountain Sheep Gather
programme.desc: Documentary following a shepherd's journey from summit to valley as he leads his sheep off Scafell Pike, reflecting on life caring for his flock in this rugged landscape. Maxine Peake reads specially commissioned poetry written by Mark Pajak to provide a counterpoint to the shepherd's insights throughout the film.(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Neil Oliver discovers how the North Sea coast keeps the Danes happy and Dick Strawbridge gets access to the construction of one of the world's largest offshore wind farms. Miranda Krestovnikoff meets some unflappable red deer, who manage to make themselves at home on a windswept shoreline despite the fact that they share the sand dunes with tanks from the Danish arm.(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Beautiful Eggs: The Genius of Carl Faberge
programme.desc: Stephen Smith explores the life and work of the renowned jeweller, best known for the eggs he made out of precious metals and gemstones for the last tsars of Russia. The presenter talks to Prince Michael of Kent about Faberge items in the Royal Collection, and to Russian oligarch Viktor Vekselberg, who spent $100million acquiring nine of the trinkets. He also follows in Faberge's footsteps, from the Green Vaults in Dresden to the corridors of the Kremlin museum, to discover how a modest family business was turned into the world's most famous supplier of luxury items.(n)

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Cromwell manoeuvres a bill through Parliament acknowledging Henry as head of the Church of England, the first step in enabling the king to grant his own divorce from Katherine of Aragon. The Boleyn family also looks to the lawyer to deal with Harry Percy, whose wife claims her own marriage is unlawful on the grounds that her husband had previously made a binding contract of marriage with Anne Boleyn. This is a task Cromwell approaches with relish, remembering how Percy helped bring down Wolsey. Historical drama, starring Mark Rylance, Damian Lewis and Claire Foy.(n)

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Henry does little to hide his disappointment when his queen gives birth to a girl, with Anne aware that her power in court rests on producing a male heir. With the Tudor line remaining vulnerable, Cromwell decrees that everyone in public life must take an oath to recognise Henry's supremacy as head of the English Church, as well as the legality of his marriage to Anne Boleyn, but Thomas More refuses to swear allegiance and is committed to the Tower. Mark Rylance, Claire Foy, Damian Lewis and Anton Lesser star.(n)

programme.title: Hilary Mantel: Return to Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Profile of author Hilary Mantel in the six months leading up to the publication of The Mirror and the Light, the final novel in her award-winning Tudor trilogy telling the story of the extraordinary rise and sudden fall of Thomas Cromwell. Hilary reveals why she embarked on the writing of the trilogy and how it has changed her life. She also delves into her past and how she grew up with a dark family secret.(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of Books
programme.sub-title: The Secret Life of Books: Cider with Rosie
programme.desc: Best-selling author Joanna Trollope traces the roots of her favourite book to uncover how Laurie Lee blended fact and fiction in his wistful elegy to a disappeared rural world.(n)

programme.title: The Great Mountain Sheep Gather
programme.desc: Documentary following a shepherd's journey from summit to valley as he leads his sheep off Scafell Pike, reflecting on life caring for his flock in this rugged landscape. Maxine Peake reads specially commissioned poetry written by Mark Pajak to provide a counterpoint to the shepherd's insights throughout the film.(n)

programme.title: Weird Nature
programme.desc: Creatures that go to extraordinary lengths to disguise or defend themselves, including the dressing crab, which adorns itself with everything from coral and seaweed to pearls and lace. Plus, the amazing hagfish which can turn water into slime by squirting mucus, trapping would-be predators.(n)

programme.title: Egypt's Lost Queens
programme.desc: Professor Joann Fletcher explores what it was like to be a woman of power in ancient Egypt, examining four of the civilisation's most powerful female rulers through a wealth of spectacular buildings, personal artefacts and amazing tombs. As well as the women who held the title of pharaoh, many others played key roles in running the state and shaping every aspect of life.(n)

programme.title: From Andy Pandy to Zebedee: The Golden Age of Children's TV
programme.desc: The story of the struggle to deliver television programmes for children, who were an often overlooked audience in the days before everything went digital. Includes interviews with pioneers of children's TV, such as Bernard Cribbins, Derek Griffiths, Janet Ellis, Johnny Ball and John Craven. Narrated by Nigel Planer.(n)

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: The Act of Supremacy has declared Henry the supreme head of the church in England, but Holy Roman emperor Charles V and his ambassador Eustache Chapuys refuse to recognise the new title or the monarch's divorce and subsequent marriage. With Anne pregnant and away from court, Henry begins to take more notice of Jane Seymour, whose family enlist Cromwell's help in their dealings with the king - much to Anne's displeasure. Historical drama, starring Mark Rylance, Damian Lewis, Clare Foy and Mathieu Amalric.(n)

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Anne accuses Cromwell of betrayal when she finds out he tried to protect Mary and not Elizabeth at a time of crisis, but the queen's power is dissolving rapidly and her enemies are gathering. Her sister-in-law Jane Rochford reveals to Cromwell that Mark Smeaton, Francis Weston and Henry Norris have all declared their love to Anne, which is a treasonable offence, and further insinuates that her husband George Boleyn's unhealthy sexual appetite extends to his sister. Historical drama, starring Mark Rylance, Claire Foy and Jessica Raine.(n)

programme.title: My Life
programme.sub-title: My Life: The Boy on the Bicycle
programme.desc: A guided tour by 16-year-old Syrian boy, Ahmed, through one of the biggest refugee camps in the world - Zaatari in Jordan. Ahmed is like any other boy his age, except he's had to leave everything behind, including his toys and technology, to move to the refugee camp. Life in Zaatari is nothing compared to home. When the Syrian civil war began in 2012 his family had to abandon their affluent lives and flee across the border to Zaatari camp with thousands of others for safety. Now living in a storage container for a home after his house was destroyed in the civil war, he has lived in Zaatari for three years, along with 80,000 other refugees, who can't leave the camp without permission. Despite their camp having everything they need, from shops to a hospital, school and football pitches, life is very different to how it was before. Ahmed doesn't let this affect his positivity though, and he finds new ways to have fun with his friends - even if it is in a desert sandstorm followed by flood.(n)

programme.desc: Documentary series following life at Belfast Harbour, with access to a hidden world of crane drivers, boat pilots, tour guides and mega ships. Elsewhere, Robert and Karen, two of the shipyard's workers, tell their stories.(n)

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by friend and Michelin-starred chef Mark Jordan, riding around Jersey on mini motorbikes while exploring the island's culinary delights. James samples one of Jersey's most famous exports - the Jersey Royal potato - which he uses to cook up a squid risotto with a difference. Mark gets a lesson in oyster shucking and James uses Jersey black butter to create a black butter tartlet.(n)

programme.title: Ainsley's Mediterranean Cookbook
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott's tour of Sardinia continues in the Nuoro region, where his guide Claudio takes him to meet his parents, who run a small bakery specialising in pane carasau, a thin crispy bread found all over the island. Claudio then takes the chef to his family's citrus farm to discover local fruit the pompia, a citrus so bitter it is never eaten raw but makes a fabulous liqueur. Moving up to the hills, Ainsley cooks a traditional sheep stew with a group of shepherds, visits one of the island's ancient archaeological sites and cooks up a Sardinian-style minestrone.(n)

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen

programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team head to the Yorkshire Dales to surprise Jim Thompson, a terminally ill 65-year-old grandfather and fundraiser. Inspired by his bravery and dedication, the makeover team decide to turn Jim's overgrown and dangerous plot into a classic cottage garden with a Yorkshire twist.(n)

programme.title: Quiz
programme.desc: Major Charles Ingram doesn't like quizzes, but for his wife Diana and brother-in-law Adrian knowing the answer is a way of life. As Diana and Adrian's repeated attempts to get into the hotseat on Who Wants to Be a Millionare? fall short, all eyes turn to an unsuspecting Charles to win the million-pound prize. Meanwhile, the repeated reappearance of middle-class, professional quizzers has not gone unnoticed by the ITV team and producer Paul Smith makes a vow to stop them by any means necessary. programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by friend and Michelin-starred chef Mark Jordan, riding around Jersey on mini motorbikes while exploring the island's culinary delights. James samples one of Jersey's most famous exports - the Jersey Royal potato - which he uses to cook up a squid risotto with a difference. Mark gets a lesson in oyster shucking and James uses Jersey black butter to create a black butter tartlet.(n)

programme.title: Ainsley's Mediterranean Cookbook
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott's tour of Sardinia continues in the Nuoro region, where his guide Claudio takes him to meet his parents, who run a small bakery specialising in pane carasau, a thin crispy bread found all over the island. Claudio then takes the chef to his family's citrus farm to discover local fruit the pompia, a citrus so bitter it is never eaten raw but makes a fabulous liqueur. Moving up to the hills, Ainsley cooks a traditional sheep stew with a group of shepherds, visits one of the island's ancient archaeological sites and cooks up a Sardinian-style minestrone.(n)

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner make a chocolate cake out of leftover Easter eggs, and provide recipes for lamb fritters and scrambled eggs. Gino D'Acampo cooks seabass in Sardinia, Levi Roots makes spicy roast lamb, and The Great British Bake Off's Jane Beedle demonstrates cherry and pistachio tart.(n)

programme.title: Griff's Great Australian Adventure

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The murder of Midsomer resident and biscuit factory boss Eric Calder leads Barnaby and Nelson to Copenhagen. The pair join forces with local female detectives Birgitte Poulsen and Anna Degn in the hunt for the killer, and during the course of their investigation they discover the businessman had numerous links to the capital. Anglo-Danish collaboration originally shown to mark Midsomer's 100th episode, guest starring Ann Eleonora Jorgensen (The Killing), Birgitte Hjort Sorensen (Borgen), Sanjeev Bhaskar and Joanna Scanlan, alongside Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee.(n)

programme.title: Morecambe and Wise Forever
programme.desc: Part one of two. Celebrating the life and work of the double act. Eric's children Gary and Gail Morecambe take a nostalgic tour of their father's birthplace and visit the house where he grew up for the first time. Eric's wife Joan tells the story of their meeting and marriage and film star Suzanne Lloyd recalls working on film That Riviera Touch.(n)

programme.title: Morecambe and Wise Forever
programme.desc: Part two of two. programme.title: The Comedy Years
programme.desc: A look back at TV comedy from 1979. It was a pivotal year for Britain, with a new prime minister in Margaret Thatcher following mass strikes in the so-called 'Winter of Discontent'. Television saw a return to political satire, with the BBC series Not the Nine O'Clock News, though the northern club scene was dominating comedic output at the time with the likes of The Cannon & Ball Show and the stand-up series The Comedians. Narrated by Hugh Bonneville.(n)

programme.desc: The council of a seaside town is in dire need of a means to attract tourists to its resort, so announces a beauty contest. While the conniving organiser plots a series of publicity stunts, an outraged feminist group plans to sabotage the event. Comedy, starring Sid James, June Whitfield, Joan Sims, Barbara Windsor, Bernard Bresslaw and Peter Butterworth.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Cleo (1964)
programme.desc: A captured Briton is mistaken for a heroic swordsman and promoted to the Roman Royal Guard, with orders to protect Julius Caesar. But the last thing he and the fated emperor expect is an assassination attempt from within Caesar's own circle of friends. Comedy, starring Amanda Barrie, Kenneth Williams, Sid James, Kenneth Connor, Jim Dale, Charles Hawtrey and Joan Sims.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Screaming (1966)
programme.desc: A sinister boffin, his sultry sister and two genetic misfits kidnap innocent women from spooky Hocomb Woods and turn them into mannequins - but two dim-witted detectives look set to throw a spanner in the works. Comedy horror, starring Kenneth Williams, Fenella Fielding, Harry H Corbett, Charles Hawtrey, Jim Dale, Joan Sims and Bernard Bresslaw.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Rachel's informant leads police to a drugs shipment at a deserted cove. The perpetrator's girlfriend, having struck up a friendship with Gina at antenatal classes, agrees to use her to help him escape - but a hostage crisis ensues, leaving the barmaid in great danger. Peggy comes up with alternative transport when Bernie worries his hearse won't be ready for a funeral. Tim Brooke-Taylor makes a guest appearance.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Jewel Robbery at the Grand Metropolitan
programme.desc: On doctor's orders, the dapper Belgian sleuth visits Brighton for what he hopes will be a relaxing holiday, but it leads to one of the most absorbing cases he has ever faced - the disappearance of a cache of jewels. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Hermione Norris, Philip Jackson and Sorcha Cusack.(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The detective is approached by a former mentor from his college days, who asks Morse to ensure the safety of his wife. However, the assignment leaves him contemplating his future with the police force. Meanwhile, a murder in the world of fashion stirs up old gangland conflicts in the city. Detective drama series, starring Shaun Evans as the young Inspector Morse.(n)

programme.title: Hatton Garden
programme.desc: What was supposed to be a sleepy Easter Bank holiday weekend in London's world-famous jewellery quarter is thrown into chaos, when Brian Reader leads a gang of career criminals in an attempt to carry out the daring task of breaking into the vault of the Hatton Garden Safe Deposit Company. Fact-based drama about the April 2015 heist, starring Timothy Spall, Kenneth Cranham, David Hayman and Alex Norton.(n)

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: The year 1956 finds jazz-loving clergyman Sidney Chambers attending a talk by Reverend Nathaniel Todd, a member of the formative US civil rights movement. During the event, protestors disrupt the speech and release fireworks, causing the audience to panic. In the chaos, Todd's son Charles is stabbed and killed by an unseen assailant. The murder sees racial tensions spike and Geordie Keating is called in to investigate. Things are further complicated when Sidney finds himself drawn to passionate young activist, and their tentative romance faces pressure from all sides. Robson Green stars, with James Norton, Tessa Peake-Jones, Samuel West and Paterson Joseph.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Adventure of the Italian Nobleman
programme.desc: Captain Hastings and the Belgian supersleuth investigate a baffling gangland murder which draws them both into the underworld of London's Little Italy. Miss Lemon turns out to have a link with the victim's valet. Whodunit, starring David Suchet and Hugh Fraser.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack learns he is tipped for promotion with the uniform branch and even more flattered to discover he has a secret admirer, but before he has time to bask in this rare moment of glory a child murder claims his attention. Linking the disappearance of a 10-year-old boy to a mysterious hit-and-run killing, he is soon mired in a complex investigation and fuming about a bureaucratic blunder that leaves a known paedophile walking free on his patch. David Jason stars.(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiona panics over keeping the salon going. Samir receives good news from the doctors about becoming a donor. Jon tells Ken that Denise has left and taken Daniel with her.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre, Samir and Tracy struggle for space in Ken's flat. Martin finds out the results of his nursing exams. Fiona calls in her friend Maxine to help out at the salon.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe wants to carry on as normal after the attack, but Emma wants her to report it. Sam gets fed up of being bossed around by Eric. Frank confronts Ken Adlington.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim is worried that Frank has found out about her and Dave. Sam enlists Butch to help with Eric's house clearance. Zoe makes a statement to the police about the attack.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Rachel's informant leads police to a drugs shipment at a deserted cove.
