Peggy comes up with alternative transport when Bernie worries his hearse won't be ready for a funeral. Tim Brooke-Taylor makes a guest appearance.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Dervish and Co are back at the crease but with ball tampering and rule bending rife, a fair game seems out of the question. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Roger worries about who will make the team's teas when Mim is taken ill. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Alan and Philip goad Rigsby into fasting for three days - and it is not long before he is stealing Vienna's cat food to stave off the pangs of hunger. With Leonard Rossiter and Richard Beckinsale.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack returns to duty after a period of suspension, and finds himself investigating the murder of a man who had been leading a double life. In addition, he also has to deal with the baffling case of a man found dead with his right foot hacked off and replaced by a severed left foot. Drama, starring David Jason and Amanda Root.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Geoff fails to control of a violent outbreak at the bookmakers, and is in awe of Joe as he takes command of the situation. Debt collector Mick Revill (guest star Martin Kemp) suggests a way to toughen him up, but elsewhere Revill is accused by a local businessman of being involved in some dubious deals. Meanwhile, Peggy can't understand David's reluctance to attend a centenary party at the school.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Britt and Terry get packed up and ready for their move. Eric's plans for the trip to France worry Frank. A fierce argument erupts between Britt and Terry at their leaving party.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: A double decker bus arrives outside the Woolpack, ready for the trip to France. Frank is arrested for assaulting Ken Adlington. The bus breaks down putting the trip in jeopardy.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Don and Josie plan an intimate weekend together. Tracy tells Ken she wishes Deirdre had never married Samir. Martin secretly plans a family caravanning holiday.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Martin and Gail are horrified to find the Duckworths in the caravan next to them. Percy, Phyllis and Betty reminisce about VE Day. Fiona worries about Denise's whereabouts.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: During a music masterclass led by a renowned pianist, a shy prodigy suffers a panic attack and runs off to a nearby river. To her horror, she witnesses a woman drowning and a baby left on the riverbank. But when Barnaby arrives, there is no sign of the victim - and he is struck by similarities to another mysterious case that happened 20 years ago at the same spot. Guest starring veteran actor James Fox and Coronation Street's Ian Puleston-Davies (Owen Armstrong), alongside former Corrie star Richard Fleeshman (Craig Harris).(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Geoff fails to control of a violent outbreak at the bookmakers, and is in awe of Joe as he takes command of the situation. Debt collector Mick Revill (guest star Martin Kemp) suggests a way to toughen him up, but elsewhere Revill is accused by a local businessman of being involved in some dubious deals. Meanwhile, Peggy can't understand David's reluctance to attend a centenary party at the school.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Adventure of the Clapham Cook
programme.desc: The dapper Belgian detective, who usually only accepts cases of national importance, bends his rules for once and finds himself in run-down Clapham. He investigates the disappearance of a local cook and becomes intrigued by the contents of the woman's trunk. David Suchet stars, alongside Hugh Fraser and Brigit Forsyth.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: At the Luxton Deeping Crime Festival, the unveiling of a newly discovered novel by deceased Midsomer crime writer George Summersbee is jeopardised when the manuscript is stolen and local artist Suzie Colebrooke is electrocuted by a booby-trapped roulette wheel. Can new father Barnaby untangle a web of jealousy and obsession to find the killer? With Fiona Dolman, Tamzin Malleson, Liberty Mills, Una Stubbs and Georgia Taylor.(n)

programme.title: Hatton Garden
programme.desc: Returning for a second attempt at the Hatton Garden safe deposit vault, the gang members, now led by Terry Perkins, set out to finish what they started. They must obtain new equipment and get back into the building, but time is getting tight with the bank holiday weekend slipping by. Drama, starring Timothy Spall and Kenneth Cranham.(n)

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: With Violet gone, Sidney loses himself in drink. After a heavy whiskey session leaves him without his jacket - and little memory of the night before - he discovers to his horror that he was the last person to see a young woman alive. As he struggles to remember her final words, Geordie grows concerned - both for the murdered girl, so callously abandoned outside a local slum, and for his friend's troubled state of mind.Â James Norton stars, with Robson Green, Tessa Peake-Jones, Al Weaver and Kacey Ainsworth.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Roger worries about who will make the team's teas when Mim is taken ill. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: As the team see victory in their grasp, Fred has a heart attack. Cricketing drama, starring Josie Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Geoff despairs as Yasmeen walks out on him, Bernie implores Chesney to keep a close eye on Gemma, and Michael attends a job interview at Underworld. David heads out for a walk, but when Gail quizzes him, he refuses to tell her where he's going.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gemma boards the bus with the quads, but is suddenly overcome by a wave of anxiety and hurries off the bus, leaving her babies behind. The return of Rick's daughter Kelly rattles Gary, and Yasmeen consults the police about Geoff's past.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve regrets prioritising Emma's needs over Oliver's, Gemma returns home and finally admits to Chesney that she thinks she's a terrible mother and no longer feels in control, while Ken decides to challenge Charles' supremacy.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Malone quietly seethes when the police discover his 4x4 smoldering in a field, Chas railroads Belle into attending Take a Vow's lingerie party, and Mandy encourages Bob to tell Wendy how he really feels.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jamie explains to Paddy that the only way to solve his money problems is to fire Belle, Al and Priya share a passionate kiss and Malone puts Billy in a difficult position.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy insists on planning Lydia's hen night, as well as Sam's stag, but Lydia is unsure. Paddy agrees to Jamie's plan and Rodney struggles running the cafÃ©.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Up the Jungle (1970)
programme.desc: A Victorian explorer recalls how he was recruited by an aristocrat to find her missing son in the African jungle. Teaming up with a fearless hunter, he hoped to find a rare bird on his journey - but instead fell into the clutches of an all-female tribe. Comedy, starring Sid James, Frankie Howerd, Joan Sims, Kenneth Connor, Charles Hawtrey and Terry Scott.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Matron (1972)
programme.desc: A wily thief gathers a band of crooks for a plot to break into a maternity hospital and steal the supply of contraceptive pills. One of the gang gets the lie of the land by disguising himself as a nurse, but the hypochondriac head doctor and formidable matron prove constant obstacles to the heist. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams and Hattie Jacques.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Camping (1969)
programme.desc: Two men lure their unsuspecting girlfriends to a nudist camp, with hopes of having a wild old time - but the joke is on the men when it turns out they have ended up at a traditional family campsite. Bawdy comedy, starring Sid James, Bernard Bresslaw, Kenneth Williams, Joan Sims and Barbara Windsor.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Up the Khyber (1968)
programme.desc: British forces in colonial India find themselves in deep trouble when a manic local ruler discovers the soldiers' fearsome reputation is thoroughly undeserved - prompting open rebellion. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey, Roy Castle, Joan Sims, Bernard Bresslaw and Terry Scott.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Case of the Missing Will
programme.desc: The sleuth sets out to solve the baffling murder of a Cambridge don who met with foul play shortly after deciding to change his will. Starring David Suchet, Beth Goddard and Mark Kingston.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The murder of Midsomer resident and biscuit factory boss Eric Calder leads Barnaby and Nelson to Copenhagen. The pair join forces with local female detectives Birgitte Poulsen and Anna Degn in the hunt for the killer, and during the course of their investigation they discover the businessman had numerous links to the capital. Anglo-Danish collaboration originally shown to mark Midsomer's 100th episode, guest starring Ann Eleonora Jorgensen (The Killing), Birgitte Hjort Sorensen (Borgen), Sanjeev Bhaskar and Joanna Scanlan, alongside Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee.(n)

programme.title: Morecambe and Wise Forever
programme.desc: Part one of two. Celebrating the life and work of the double act. Eric's children Gary and Gail Morecambe take a nostalgic tour of their father's birthplace and visit the house where he grew up for the first time. Eric's wife Joan tells the story of their meeting and marriage and film star Suzanne Lloyd recalls working on film That Riviera Touch.(n)

programme.title: Morecambe and Wise Forever
programme.desc: Part two of two. Anita Harris - one of Eric and Ernie's guest stars from the TV series - looks back at rare behind-the-scenes footage of herself in rehearsal, Eric's son Gary makes an emotional trip back to the family villa in Portugal, and Eric's other son Steven recalls fishing with Eric on the River Test.(n)

programme.title: The Comedy Years
programme.desc: A look back at TV comedy from 1979. It was a pivotal year for Britain, with a new prime minister in Margaret Thatcher following mass strikes in the so-called 'Winter of Discontent'. Television saw a return to political satire, with the BBC series Not the Nine O'Clock News, though the northern club scene was dominating comedic output at the time with the likes of The Cannon & Ball Show and the stand-up series The Comedians. Narrated by Hugh Bonneville.(n)

programme.title: The Syndicate
programme.desc: Jamie lives the high life, convinced the police cannot touch him now he has money - but the past comes back to haunt him when the local drug boss pays a visit. Bob says goodbye to those he loves as he prepares to undergo surgery, and Stuart is torn between his life with Amy and supporting Leanne, who is getting ready for her court case in Wales. Timothy Spall, Matthew McNulty, Joanna Page and Matthew Lewis star.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Doctor (1967)
programme.desc: A popular doctor is sacked after a misunderstanding ruins his reputation, prompting the furious patients to stage a revolt against the luckless medic's strait-laced superiors. Meanwhile, the hospital head has his own problems after becoming the unlikely romantic target of a strict matron. Comedy, with Frankie Howerd, Jim Dale, Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey and Hattie Jacques.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Matron (1972)
programme.desc: A wily thief gathers a band of crooks for a plot to break into a maternity hospital and steal the supply of contraceptive pills. One of the gang gets the lie of the land by disguising himself as a nurse, but the hypochondriac head doctor and formidable matron prove constant obstacles to the heist. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams and Hattie Jacques.(n)

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: Vanessa fears for the animals at Mara during a rabies outbreak at the National Park. Danny is asked to bring it under control, but during the quarantine operation he clashes with feisty vet Alice, who has been sent to help him. Evan is thrown a party to celebrate his return to Leopard's Den and Rosie is surprised when elephant calf Tula warms to Charlotte. Starring Stephen Tompkinson.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Malone quietly seethes when the police discover his 4x4 smoldering in a field, Chas railroads Belle into attending Take a Vow's lingerie party, and Mandy encourages Bob to tell Wendy how he really feels.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jamie explains to Paddy that the only way to solve his money problems is to fire Belle, Al and Priya share a passionate kiss and Malone puts Billy in a difficult position.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy insists on planning Lydia's hen night, as well as Sam's stag, but Lydia is unsure. Paddy agrees to Jamie's plan and Rodney struggles running the cafÃ©.(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Miss Jones reports a prowler to the police and it is not long before an inspector calls to investigate - but, unfortunately for Rigsby, he is not all he seems. George Sewell guest stars in the classic comedy, with Leonard Rossiter and Frances de la Tour.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Tongues are set wagging when a few unlikely liaisons are formed at the Brent Park barbecue. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Mutiny on the Buses (1972)
programme.desc: Chirpy bus driver Stan Butler lands himself in trouble as he struggles to raise enough money to marry his girlfriend, and on top of training his unemployed brother-in-law for a job at the depot he has to contend with an officious new manager. Comedy based on the sitcom, starring Reg Varney, Stephen Lewis, Bob Grant, Michael Robbins, Anna Karen and Doris Hare.(n)

programme.title: The Great St Trinian's Train Robbery (1966)
programme.desc: A crook and his cronies stash the money from a train heist in a deserted old mansion, planning to recover it in good time. However, several years later, when the thieves arrive to collect their loot, they discover the building has been turned into a school for girls where the pupils have become infamous for their escapades. Comedy, starring Frankie Howerd, Dora Bryan, Reg Varney and George Cole.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Cruising (1962)
programme.desc: The captain of a cruise ship bound for the Mediterranean is less than impressed when five of his regular shipmates are replaced at the last minute. Despite their best efforts to earn his respect and an eagerness to please, the new recruits seem doomed to cause chaos whatever they do. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Liz Fraser and Kenneth Connor.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Girls (1973)
programme.desc: The council of a seaside town is in dire need of a means to attract tourists to its resort, so announces a beauty contest. While the conniving organiser plots a series of publicity stunts, an outraged feminist group plans to sabotage the event. Comedy, starring Sid James, June Whitfield, Joan Sims, Barbara Windsor, Bernard Bresslaw and Peter Butterworth.(n)

Can new father Barnaby untangle a web of jealousy and obsession to find the killer? With Fiona Dolman, Tamzin Malleson, Liberty Mills, Una Stubbs and Georgia Taylor.(n)

programme.title: Hatton Garden
programme.desc: Returning for a second attempt at the Hatton Garden safe deposit vault, the gang members, now led by Terry Perkins, set out to finish what they started. They must obtain new equipment and get back into the building, but time is getting tight with the bank holiday weekend slipping by. Drama, starring Timothy Spall and Kenneth Cranham.(n)

programme.title: The Car Chasers
programme.desc: The team considers setting up shop in California as Jeff and Meg travel to LA to check out potential sites. Once there, Jeff is unable to resist the chance to make the trip pay for itself by trying to snap up a couple of cars - a Lamborghini Countach and a Porsche Speedster.(n)

programme.title: Auto Mundial
programme.desc: Featuring the new McLaren 765LT and a roofless Aston Martin.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle begin an investigation into why a city's crime rate is steadily falling. They later find themselves victimised by local officers after being charged with a minor traffic offence. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur's prestige as a pillar of the community is boosted by an invitation to address a meeting of his local Rotary Club - which is being held in the very hotel where Terry is acting as temporary house detective. However, a pair of thieves posing as priests and a mangeress equally as crooked as Arthur threaten to ruin his grand moment. Drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry intervenes in a tug-of-love dispute concerning an Anglo-Italian Mancini family, and finds himself unwittingly threatening the success of Arthur's latest money-making venture - a health club. Dennis Waterman and George Cole star.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A crook arranges the conversion of replica firearms into the real thing and sells them on to small-time hoodlums. When one of the arms misfires killing both a policeman and its holder, Regan is intent on discovering the source before more innocent lives are lost. Crime drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A frustrated housewife supplies Regan with the names of the gang who recently ransacked a bookmakers - including that of her husband, a notorious robber. However, he has a cast-iron alibi, so proving his guilt proves harder than anticipated. Crime drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan encounters a former nemesis at a party, who drunkenly admits involvement in an earlier robbery in which he was let go due to lack of evidence. He disapproves of his daughter's villainous boyfriend, and Regan suspects he is being exploited to help break the romantic union. However, an even darker plan is being hatched, with the daughter an innocent pawn in a robbery. Kenny Lynch and Derek Francis guest star, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: The Train Robbers (1973)
programme.desc: A thief's grieving widow persuades a gunslinger to help her find a cache of her husband's stolen gold and return it to the rightful owners. However, a group of Mexican bandits and a mysterious stranger are also after the loot. Western, starring John Wayne, Ann-Margret, Rod Taylor and Ben Johnson.(n)

programme.title: The Sting (1973)
programme.desc: Two conmen decide to swindle a mobster out of a fortune in revenge for the murder of a mutual friend. Enlisting the help of a host of crooks, they quickly conjure up a brilliant scheme to make him pay for his crime. Oscar-winning Depression-era comedy drama, starring Robert Redford, Paul Newman, Robert Shaw, Charles Durning and Eileen Brennan.(n)

programme.title: Jaws: The Revenge (1987)

programme.title: Under Siege 2: Dark Territory (1995)
programme.desc: A retired Navy veteran travelling by train is forced to leap into action when he realises terrorist hijackers are on board. Action adventure sequel, with Steven Seagal, Eric Bogosian, Morris Chestnut, Brenda Bakke and Katherine Heigl.(n)

programme.title: Red River (1948)
programme.desc: Conflict grows between a tough Texan rancher and his Civil War veteran foster son during a 1,000 mile cattle drive to Missouri. The older man's uncompromising methods and unyielding outlook lead his workers to mutiny - and the son ends up siding with the rebellious ranch hands against his father. Howard Hawks' Western, starring John Wayne, Montgomery Clift, Walter Brennan, Joanne Dru and Coleen Gray.(n)

programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle investigate the death of a burglar who was killed after seeing classified information about a foreign country, and resort to theft in a bid to uncover the embassy's sinister secrets. Liz Fraser and Michael Elphick guest star.(n)

programme.desc: Conflict grows between a tough Texan rancher and his Civil War veteran foster son during a 1,000 mile cattle drive to Missouri. programme.desc: Jamie Oliver demonstrates more vibrant, hearty and healthy vegetarian dishes that everyone can add to their menus, finding inspiration from across the country and around the globe. In the spice shops of Jerusalem, he seeks meat-free inspiration and cooks up a show-stopping cauliflower roast.(n)

programme.title: Jamie's Meat-Free Meals
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver makes a chilli that proves vegetarian dishes can pack a punch, while in India he learns new tricks and creates a spiced parsnip soup full of texture and surprise. He is also inspired by traditional and modern street food in the Middle East as he rustles up mouthwatering meat-free pasties.(n)

programme.title: Sunday Brunch
programme.desc: Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer present a special edition of the food and chat show, with celebrity guest interviews and music performances conducted online with guests including Nish Kumar, Steph McGovern, Joe Lycett and Fleur East.(n)

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer becomes a bounty hunter to earn extra money and hires an unlikely assistant in the form of Ned Flanders - who ends up pursuing him on a charge of skipping bail over a trivial offence. Meanwhile, Marge takes a job at an unusual bakery.(n)

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart finds himself in trouble when he steals a mobile phone from Denis Leary and subsequently uses it to land the actor a comical film role. The trouble-maker then leads his family to the ancient ruins of Machu Picchu after Marge confiscates the device and decides to use its GPS technology to track her son.(n)

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: An irreverent take on the Prince and the Pauper. Bart enthusiastically trades lives with wealthy lookalike Simon Woosterfield after landing himself in trouble. Simon settles into life at home with the Simpsons, but Bart finds the lavish lifestyle too much to handle when he becomes the target of all his new friends' pranks.(n)

programme.title: We're Going on a Bear Hunt
programme.desc: Animated adventure, based on the popular children's bedtime book by Michael Rosen and Helen Oxenbury. Stan, Katie, Rosie, Max and the baby, along with Rufus the dog, all embark on a quest to find some bears. Trekking through whirling snowstorms, muddy bogs and dark forests, the siblings refuse to let anything get in their way. However, when Rosie and Rufus end up getting lost, bear-hunting suddenly doesn't seem quite so much fun after all. Featuring the voices of Olivia Colman, Pam Ferris and Mark Williams as Mum, Grandma and Dad respectively.(n)

programme.title: The Tiger Who Came to Tea
programme.desc: Hand-drawn animated version of Judith Kerr's much-loved tale, which celebrated its 50th anniversary in 2018. A tea-guzzling tiger turns up unannounced and eats and drinks young Sophie and her mummy out of everything in the house, even draining the water from their taps. Narrated by David Walliams, with David Oyelowo providing the voice for the big cat alongside seven-year-old Clara Ross as Sophie, with other characters played by Benedict Cumberbatch, Tamsin Greig and Paul Whitehouse.(n)

programme.title: Percy Jackson: Sea of Monsters (2013)

programme.desc: The food writer travels to Cambodia with her adopted daughter Li-Da, in the hope of tracking down her biological family. programme.desc: Documentary following the struggle of three severely morbidly obese people as they attempt to regain control of their lives before it is too late. Claire has been bed-bound for the last six years. Trapped by her 40-stone body, she can only see the world through the window of the room where she spends her life. Claire has two daughters - Tracey and Jasmine - who need to do nearly everything for her. Tracey has two young children and a home of her own, so the burden of care falls mainly on 19-year-old Jasmine. Realising she may be useful in helping him keep up a successful appearance, he pays her to accompany him for a week as he associates with the rich and powerful - but falls in love despite their many differences. Romantic comedy, starring Richard Gere and Julia Roberts, with Ralph Bellamy and Laura San Giacomo.(n)

programme.title: My Posh Date
programme.desc: One-off show in which people from opposite ends of Britain's class system go on a series of dates to find out if love can bridge the divide. First up are gas fitter Jack and trainee barrister Holly. She introduces him to her favourite pastime, horse riding, while he introduces her to his mum, who worries about her son going out with a posh girl. Meanwhile, prosperous Sam takes teacher Amy on a picnic and punting extravaganza - but will her passion for kebabs prove too much for her privileged would-be partner?(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Botched Up Bodies
programme.desc: Former boxing promoter Kellie Maloney was left fighting for her life after a second round of facial feminisation to make her face more womanly. Former model Linsey Dawn McKenzie managed to receive an unintended facelift, which left her with no earlobes and huge scars. Plus, the programme also follows La Toya Jackson's surgical journey.(n)

programme.title: My Mum's Hotter Than Me!
programme.desc: Former glamour model Sam maintains her looks with regular Botox, even though she's about to become a grandmother. However, she confesses she would hate her two daughters to follow her lead. Plus, a professional escort feels under pressure from her mother to take up glamour modelling.(n)

programme.title: House Doctor

programme.title: Great Artists
programme.desc: Seventeenth-century Dutch painter Jan Vermeer, the lowly son of an innkeeper and art dealer, lived an uneventful life, leaving fewer than 40 authenticated works and dying unknown outside his home town. Tim Marlow investigates how his talent came to be regarded as highly as Rembrandt's, and examines the simple domestic scenes that led to his posthumous rise to fame.(n)

programme.title: Wildlife SOS
programme.desc: The team treats an owl that refuses to fly and a badger with life-threatening injuries, while Simon tries to free a deer that has become stuck in railings.(n)

Once there, she befriends a sweet-natured teacher who convinces her that not all grown-ups are bad - and urges her to use her special powers to turn the tables on the nasty headmistress. Fantasy comedy, adapted from the children's book by Roald Dahl, with Mara Wilson, Pam Ferris, Embeth Davidtz, Rhea Perlman and Danny DeVito, who also directed.(n)

programme.title: The Bridge on the River Kwai (1957)
programme.desc: A British colonel in a Japanese PoW camp is ordered to build a railway bridge over a river, forging a vital communications link. At first he refuses to obey, but eventually agrees to take the project on - and becomes so obsessed with the task that he creates a dilemma for an American soldier sent to destroy the structure. David Lean's Oscar-winning Second World War drama, starring Alec Guinness, William Holden and Jack Hawkins.(n)

programme.title: The Yorkshire Steam Railway: All Aboard
programme.desc: Under pressure to bring in extra revenue now that the somewhat challenging season is nearing its end, the North York Moors Railway team decides to put on a special 'curry train' night together with a massive bash for Halloween. Visitors and staff alike put in the effort to make it a night to remember, though proceedings are threatened when the lights in the carriages suddenly stop working. The Bishop of Wakefield comes to bless Eric Treacy, an engine that has been through a Â£500,000 overhaul taking over two years.(n)

programme.title: Around the World by Train with Tony Robinson
programme.desc: The actor rides the El Chepe train from the west coast of Mexico through to the Copper Canyon - a system larger and deeper than the Grand Canyon. From there, Tony jumps on Mexico's only passenger line to head towards the USA. Along the way, he stops in the city of Chihuahua where he discovers Sotol, the region's answer to Tequila. He then heads to Juarez, a city once strongly associated with drug cartels. After crossing the US border into El Paso, Tony meets a fellow train enthusiast trying to revive historic street cars. The final stop on this leg of his journey is Los Angeles' Union Station.(n)

programme.title: Springtime on the Farm
programme.desc: The return of the programme celebrating the hard work of farmers around the country, with Helen Skelton and Adam Henson seeing how they are responding to the Covid-19 crisis, and showing how nature carries on regardless. Working safely within the new social distancing guidelines, Helen presents from her Yorkshire country garden and Adam from his Cotswold farm, while Cannon Hall Farm's Rob and Dave Nicholson will be filming the all-important lambings themselves as they attempt to catch all of the last-minute action.(n)

programme.title: Hard Men: Street Justice

Another man reveals how he was one of the first to rob other criminals of their ill-gotten gains, and used his propensity for violence to make sure he always got the upper hand.(n)

programme.desc: The criminal brothers rise to the top of the London underworld, but face a struggle to protect their growing empire from both rivals and the police. programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley and entrepreneur Rosie Wolfenden follow hobby crafters as they get to show their creations at craft fairs for the first time, beginning with three makers taking their items to Clare Priory Craft Fair in Suffolk. Heather quit her job to devote her time to making clay fantasy figures, so she needs to start making her hobby pay. John rents a huge studio full of quilting machines and fabrics to realise his passion, and Toni is devoted to crochet. Will any of them be able to make their hobbies pay?(n)

programme.title: Dogs with Extraordinary Jobs
programme.desc: A look at the use of dogs' natural instinct to love, nurture and protect, including a blind Philadelphia woman who has found a new lease of life thanks to her new running mate and a Jack Russell caring for a surprising array of species in Namibia.(n)

programme.desc: Helen Skelton and Adam Henson continue their celebration of the hard work of farmers around the country, seeing how they are responding to the Covid-19 crisis, and showing how nature carries on regardless. Working safely within the new social distancing guidelines, Helen presents from her Yorkshire country garden and Adam from his Cotswold farm. JP Gill becomes a vet for the day, and alpacas and Stirling bulls put in an appearance.(n)

programme.title: A Country Life for Half the Price with Kate Humble
programme.desc: Kate meets the Goldby family from Epsom, where Vicki, Phil and their nine children have outgrown their four-bedroom house - which has only one bathroom. They have found a farmhouse with three bathrooms and scope for seven bedrooms, as well as more than two acres of land.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Favourite Gameshows
programme.desc: A countdown of popular game shows, featuring memorable stories from The Generation Game to The Chase. The programme remembers memorable contestants, from a cheating major in Who Wants to Be a Millionaire to a contestant on The Krypton Factor who completed the assault course with a broken leg. Featuring interviews with presenters including Gordon Burns and Vernon Kay, and contestants Judith Keppel, Richard Arnold and Kate Garraway.(n)

programme.title: Capture the Flag (2015)
programme.desc: A young surfer and his friends try to prevent a devious plan to change the course of history when a ruthless billionaire sets out to stop the moon landings from happening, so that he can gain control of a valuable energy source. Animated sci-fi adventure, with the voices of Sam Fink, Phillipa Alexander and Lorraine Pilkington.(n)

programme.title: Ice Age (2002)
programme.desc: Three mismatched prehistoric animals join forces to return a human infant to its tribe - but as their journey unfolds, the creatures fail to anticipate the many perils and pitfalls that lay in store for them. Animated adventure, featuring the voices of John Leguizamo, Ray Romano, Denis Leary, Goran Visnjic and Jack Black.(n)

programme.title: Epic (2013)
programme.desc: A teenager wanders into a forest that her scientist father believes is home to a race of miniature people. She stumbles on the tiny creatures and is shrunk down to their size so she can carry out a quest to save their new queen from an invading army. Animated adventure, with the voices of Amanda Seyfried, Josh Hutcherson, Colin Farrell and Christoph Waltz.(n)

programme.title: Step Up All In (2014)
programme.desc: A Miami street dancer faces a series of setbacks as he seeks his fortune in Hollywood. He assembles a team of dancers which prove successful in a reality TV contest broadcast from Las Vegas, but struggles to put aside old conflicts in the pursuit of his dreams. Fifth film in the dance drama series, starring Ryan Guzman and Briana Evigan.(n)

programme.title: The League of Extraordinary Gentlemen (2003)
programme.desc: Fearless hunter Allan Quatermain teams up with a host of other characters from Victorian literature - including Captain Nemo, the Invisible Man, Tom Sawyer and Dr Jekyll - to put a stop to the nefarious Fantom's evil plot to bring about a world war. Fantasy adventure, based on Alan Moore's comic-book series, starring Sean Connery, Shane West, Stuart Townsend, Richard Roxburgh, Peta Wilson and Tony Curran.(n)

programme.title: Mission: Impossible - Rogue Nation (2015)
programme.desc: The team of secret agents faces being shut down when the head of the CIA convinces the US Senate that it is too dangerous. The operatives of the Impossible Mission Force become fugitives when they defy orders and plot to bring down a shadowy society of assassins. Spy thriller sequel, starring Tom Cruise, Jeremy Renner, Rebecca Ferguson and Simon Pegg.(n)

programme.title: Prospect (2018)
programme.desc: A teenager and her father travel to a remote alien moon aiming to strike it rich, but there are others roving the wilderness and the job quickly devolves into a fight to survive. Sci-fi thriller, starring Sophie Thatcher, Jay Duplass and Pedro Pascal.(n)

programme.title: The Greasy Strangler (2016)
programme.desc: Brayden lives with his bad-tempered father Ronnie and together they run a walking tour that sheds light on their town's place in the history of disco music. On one outing father and son both fall for one of their clients and this love triangle plays out at the same time as a naked, grease-smothered serial killer is strangling his victims in the neighbourhood. Comedy horror, starring Michael St Michaels and Sky Elobar.(n)

programme.title: BSL Zone
programme.desc: In this arts documentary, Nadia Nadarajah explores the influence of BSL poet Dot Miles and the significance of her poetry.(n)

programme.title: Mary and the Witch's Flower (2017)
programme.desc: A strange flower grants a girl magic powers. Animated adventure based on The Little Broomstick by Mary Stewart, featuring the voices of Kate Winslet and Jim Broadbent. Dubbed in English.(n)

programme.title: Rise of the Guardians (2012)
programme.desc: Magical beings from folklore, including Santa Claus, Jack Frost and the Easter Bunny, join forces to defeat the plans of an evil spirit, who intends to steal the dreams of children and use the power they grant him to dominate the world. Animated adventure, with the voices of Chris Pine, Alec Baldwin, Hugh Jackman, Isla Fisher and Jude Law.(n)

programme.title: The Spiderwick Chronicles (2008)
programme.desc: Life for identical twins Jared and Simon and their sister Mallory begins afresh when their mother inherits a spooky mansion. But after moving in, strange things begin to happen, and as the youngsters investigate, they discover a book that opens up a mysterious world of amazing supernatural creatures. Fantasy adventure, starring Freddie Highmore in a dual role alongside Sarah Bolger and Nick Nolte.(n)

programme.title: Shrek the Third (2007)
programme.desc: The bad-tempered ogre finds himself in the unwelcome position of being next in line for the throne. Preferring to stay at home in the swamps, he sets out on a quest to find the rightful heir - a rebellious teenager named Artie - and persuade him to face up to his responsibilities as the future king. Meanwhile, the ambitious Prince Charming sees his chance to take the throne. Animated comedy, with the voices of Mike Myers, Cameron Diaz, Justin Timberlake, Eddie Murphy, Antonio Banderas and Rupert Everett.(n)

programme.title: Home Alone (1990)
programme.desc: A boy has to fend for himself when he is accidentally left behind after his family jets off to Paris for Christmas. The youngster initially enjoys his freedom, but is soon forced to resort to ingenious booby traps to defend the family home from two dim-witted burglars. Comedy, starring Macaulay Culkin, Joe Pesci, Daniel Stern, John Heard, Catherine O'Hara and John Candy.(n)

programme.title: Snatched (2017)
programme.desc: After being dumped by her boyfriend before their holiday, Emily Middleton takes her cautious mother Linda with her to Ecuador. However, their lives are soon turned upside down when the pair are kidnapped. Fish-out-of-water comedy, starring Amy Schumer, Goldie Hawn and Joan Cusack.(n)

programme.title: Happy-Go-Lucky (2008)
programme.desc: An eternally optimistic schoolteacher is determined to maintain an upbeat attitude whatever life throws at her and help others to do the same. However, she meets her total opposite when she takes lessons with an embittered driving instructor perpetually raging at the world. Mike Leigh's comedy drama, starring Sally Hawkins, Eddie Marsan and Alexis Zegerman.(n)

programme.title: A Million Ways to Die in the West (2014)
programme.desc: A timid farmer abandoned by his girlfriend is fascinated by a mysterious woman who rides into his frontier town. He tries to act the part of a gunslinging Wild West hero to impress her, only to find himself facing a showdown with her notorious outlaw husband. Western spoof, directed by and starring Seth MacFarlane, with Charlize Theron and Liam Neeson.(n)

programme.title: The Perfect Catch (2005)
programme.desc: Unlikely couple Ben and Lindsey meet and fall in love over the winter. When summer brings a new baseball season, Lindsey discovers Ben's devotion to the Boston Red Sox baseball team knows no bounds - and suspects she will always come second. The Farrelly brothers' romantic comedy, starring Jimmy Fallon and Drew Barrymore, inspired by Nick Hornby's football-themed novel Fever Pitch, but with America's favourite sport replacing Britain's.(n)

programme.title: The Indian in the Cupboard (1995)
programme.desc: A nine-year-old boy is given an old cupboard and a toy Iroquois Indian for his birthday - but when he puts the figure inside the cupboard, he is astonished to see it come to life. He repeats the experiment with more of his toys, and realises he is creating real people - and is responsible for their wellbeing. Children's fantasy, with Hal Scardino, Litefoot and Steve Coogan.(n)

programme.title: Ice Age: The Meltdown (2006)
programme.desc: Sid the sloth, Manny the mammoth and Diego the sabre-toothed tiger grimly realise the good life is about to come to an abrupt end as flood waters threaten their homes. However, a vulture suggests they could turn an old giant tree into a boat and make their escape, so the three friends set off in search of it. Animated adventure sequel, with the voices of Ray Romano, John Leguizamo and Denis Leary.(n)

programme.title: Harry and the Hendersons (1987)
programme.desc: A hapless family's camping holiday in the mountains is brought to a sudden end when they accidentally hit the legendary Bigfoot with their car. They decide to adopt the endearing creature, but have more trouble than expected trying to keep him concealed and to avoid arousing their neighbours' suspicions. Comedy, starring John Lithgow, Melinda Dillon, David Suchet, Don Ameche and Kevin Peter Hall.(n)

programme.title: Home Again (2017)
programme.desc: Five months after separating from her husband, Alice Kinney and her daughters relocate from New York to her childhood home in Los Angeles, which belonged to her late father, Oscar-winning film director John Kinney. Eventually things take an unexpected turn when she lets three young guys move in with her. Romantic comedy, starring Reese Witherspoon, Michael Sheen and Candice Bergen.(n)

programme.title: The Ritual (2017)
programme.desc: A group of college friends reunite for a trip to the forest, but encounter a menacing presence in the woods that is stalking them. Horror, starring Rafe Spall.(n)

programme.title: Trainspotting (1996)
programme.desc: A heroin addict makes a series of attempts to kick the habit, but the grind of everyday life and a circle of similarly self-destructive friends keep dragging him back to his old ways. Following a near-fatal overdose, he comes to the realisation that the only way to put his life in order is to rid himself of his friends - which is easier said than done. Danny Boyle's drama based on Irvine Welsh's novel, starring Ewan McGregor, Robert Carlyle and Ewen Bremner.(n)

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Drama following the lives and loves of a group of teenagers and young adults in Chester.(n)

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Hapless Liam wants his greatest passion to take flight with a darts-inspired wedding, but controlling bride-to-be Chelsey dreams of a much more traditional affair.(n)

programme.title: The Rugrats Movie (1998)
programme.desc: Tommy tries to return his new brother to the baby shop, helped by Angelica, Chuckie, Phil and Lil - but the friends become lost in dense woodlands en route, turning their expedition into a scary and exciting journey of discovery. programme.desc: Pub manager and artist Matt Wren hosts the second evening in south London, but his dinner party is beset by disasters in the kitchen and arguing guests at the dinner table when Michelle and Licia have a spat. However, one of the five takes a liking to another of the contestants.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Licia Snow hosts the third party in south London, and hopes to impress her guests with rustic and homely touches during her laid-back evening. However, her relaxed and calm approach could be threatened when she clashes with Michelle at the dinner table.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Raphael Steele hosts the fourth night in south London and hopes that his first-ever dinner party will be a success. He plans a Caribbean evening based on old family recipes featuring ackee, saltfish and curried goat, and organises steel drum music for entertainment.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Swedish businesswoman Charlotte Sundberg hosts the final party in south London. Her tactics are to present a night full of schnapps, folk songs and classic cuisine from her Scandinavian homeland, which she hopes will help her scoop the cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Claire Hilton, who runs Robertsbrook Guest House in Swanage, Dorset, feels she has a good chance of winning the title of best B&B as another round of the competition gets under way. However, guests Lynn Self and Ziggy Broome have doubts over the cleanliness of their bedroom, and another couple are accustomed to more opulent surroundings.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Lynn Self and Ziggy Broome hope to impress with their two-star B&B Eden Lodge in Falmouth, Cornwall. However, the state of their property disappoints their fellow competitors. At dinner, James and Maria Church reveal the truth about their famous daughter Charlotte, and the following morning's breakfast reduces some of the guests to fits of giggles.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: It is the turn of Charlotte Church's parents James and Maria to play host at Dexby Townhouse in Cardiff, and they pull out all the stops by laying on a day of white-water rafting and a magic performance courtesy of their nephew Elliot. But the following morning, some of the guests are kept waiting for breakfast.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Newlyweds Pip Easby and Nicky Lesser-Easby, who run Muddifords Court Country House in Devon, open their doors for the final stay of the competition. The Churches find their four-poster bed too small, and not all the guests enjoy their trip to a local glass-blowing factory, but feedback at check-out time leaves the hosts feeling confident.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances. An underpayment from a surprising source changes the dynamics of the group and throws the competition wide open, before the winner is announced.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Australian Natalie Dixon hosts the first dinner party in the Vale of Aylesbury, where she hopes that her Asian fusion menu will secure her the Â£1,000 prize. However, one of her guests - food regulation lawyer Greg Pearson - will be paying particularly close attention as he joins football stadium announcer Mike Botto, sales manager Bekki Jones and 74-year-old taxi driver John Feore at the table.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Taxi driver John Feore hosts the second night in the Vale of Aylesbury, where the 70-year-old hopes to impress his guests with a traditional Irish roast dinner, complete with all the trimmings - but the septuagenarian cabbie struggles with the complex timings of the meal.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Competitive sales manager Bekki Jones hosts the third dinner party in the Vale of Aylesbury, where she tries to keep the mood light with stories of nudity and twin telepathy - but cabbie John manages to insult construction company boss Natalie Dixon by comparing her to Miss Piggy.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Greg Pearson hosts the fourth party of the competition in the Vale of Aylesbury, Buckinghamshire, and hopes that his food knowledge will be enough to secure a successful evening. However, fellow diner Bekki Jones is out for revenge after he criticised her dessert, and his guests are left underwhelmed as he tries to charm the group.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Last to host in the Vale of Aylesbury is football match announcer Mike Botto, who hopes his Italian-themed evening will secure him victory. He tries to impress with spaghetti Bolognaise, and enlists a Pavarotti impersonator to sing for his guests. However, Australian Natalie Dixon reveals that, unlike Mike, she knows what she is talking about when it comes to Italy. The fallout between Greg Pearson and cabbie John Feore also sours the mood.(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Presenter Tim Shaw and mechanic Fuzz Townshend head to Warwickshire to try to spruce up a 1970 Lotus Elan. The car is owned by an ex-racing driver but a painful spinal injury has prevented him from carrying out maintenance on the vehicle.(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Cameras follow the paramedic teams of West Midlands Ambulance Service, beginning with a crew treating a man found collapsed in his garden in Birmingham.(n)

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: Over the last decade, incidents of violence perpetrated by young men have risen by 22 per cent and this edition examines the stories behind some of these calls and crimes. In Swindon, officers Emily Grigor and Simon Sanghera are called to arrest a man in his early twenties who has ripped off his top in a drunken rage before putting his fist through a wall and his foot through a window in a bar.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cora, 62, arrives by air ambulance following a high-speed road accident, which left her trapped in the car for more than an hour. Her husband Rae, 65, who was driving, is brought in soon after, and staff are concerned he may have sustained serious head, neck and internal injuries. Scott, 23, has badly cut his knee after a night out, and while he's being treated, he reflects on his schizophrenia diagnosis as a teenager. Retired carpenter Richard, 68, has fallen and hit his head on the pavement, but medics also want to look into his unusually low blood pressure.(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Cameras follow the paramedic teams of West Midlands Ambulance Service, beginning with a crew treating a man found collapsed in his garden in Birmingham.(n)

programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to West Nyack, New York, to come to the aid of a family-run Mexican restaurant that is suffering a financial crisis, and a case of hard-headed patriarchy. When the chef arrives he finds himself turfing out the existing customers for their own safety due to a cockroach infestation on the premises.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cora, 62, arrives by air ambulance following a high-speed road accident, which left her trapped in the car for more than an hour. Her husband Rae, 65, who was driving, is brought in soon after, and staff are concerned he may have sustained serious head, neck and internal injuries. Scott, 23, has badly cut his knee after a night out, and while he's being treated, he reflects on his schizophrenia diagnosis as a teenager. programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Jack is declared dead after drinking too much floor polish - and Ted is astounded to learn he has been left a fortune in the will. However, he has to fulfil certain conditions before collecting the money.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp visits the Chagford Show, Devon, where she enters her home-made elderflower cordial in the garden crafts competition.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Sara Damergi helps find a suitable property for Mark and Wendy Lang, a couple from Liverpool with a Â£90,000 budget who want to start a new life on Spain's Costa Almeria.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Presenter Sara Damergi helps Chris Grandison and his grandmother Patricia Blythe search for a holiday home in Malta. Patricia and her late husband lived on the island many years ago when he served in the Armed Forces, and her grandson hopes to find a property with rental potential on a budget of Â£70,000.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Max McMurdo and Henry Cole visit a motorbike fan, making table lamps from engines and turning a pallet and iron wheels into a stylish modern coffee table.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head east to Newark in search of their latest upcycling prospects. Simon finds an antique European threshing board, which he turns into a coffee table, and Henry's all at sea with a ship's porthole.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Stephanie prepares to end the year in style by throwing a Christmas dinner to remember. However, with the ancient range cooker out of action and the 19th-century bread oven put into service cooking the turkey, it's a tall order to deliver a five-course extravaganza for 17 guests. Angel sets Dick the task of finding and chopping the perfect logs for her Christmas table display in their 12-acre grounds. Christmas cheer threatens to run dry for south Londoners Lee and Belinda as freezing temperatures and a broken boiler put their first ever festive event in peril.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at Wharfe House in Wetherby, run by B&B newcomer Pauline Cox, who's joined by her best friend Claire Smith. Eyebrows are raised when Mike and Karen Hepker arrive from Bath Lodge Castle in a chauffeur-driven car. During a visit to a local vineyard, sparks fly between Mike Hepker and Tash Thorne, who runs The Old Lodge Hotel in Gosport with her partner Mike Pearce. Having only just opened, Pauline has a house full for the very first time. The narrow antique beds cause concerns and Jenny and Clive Aylett from Wortwell Hall Barn in Norfolk are first to criticise breakfast. When it comes to feedback, Pauline's left deflated by her sausage scores - and Karen and Mike Hepker vote with their feet.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners visit Wortwell Hall Barn in Norfolk, run by husband-and-wife duo Clive and Jenny Aylett, but when the pair's rivals arrive, Pauline Cox and Claire Smith have difficulty locating their favourite appliance. Publicans Mike Pearce and Tash Thorne are astonished to discover they cannot lock their room's door. In the morning, some of the guest make awkward requests to change their breakfast orders, causing professional chef Clive to experience a meltdown in the kitchen that leads to a fundamental error.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to Bath Lodge Castle in Somerset for the third day of the competition, where they are wowed by Karen and Mike Hepker's luxury castle experience. Although the guests are disappointed at the bathroom fixtures and fittings, they are given the chance to vent their emotions during an afternoon of painting in the castle grounds. Plus, tensions rise between Mike and Tash at dinner, and harsh comments at feedback lead the hosts to suspect game playing.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to The Old Lodge Hotel in Gosport, Hampshire, run by publicans Mike Pearce and Tash Thorne. Karen Hepker is disappointed with the honeymoon suite, while Clive and Jenny Aylett find a dubious looking splash guard in the shower. A boat tour backfires when Jenny struggles with her water phobia and at dinner Karen takes the opportunity to tell the others exactly what she thinks of them. But things really kick off when it comes to feedback time.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Wharfe House hosts Pauline Cox and Claire Smith begin a bitter debate, demanding to know why their beds caused such a sleepless night. Accusations subsequently fly in all directions and walk-outs look on the cards as tensions mount leading to the payments being revealed.(n)

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: A family leave London to run a gite in a tiny French village, while two best friends try to beat the competition as they open a bar on one of Spain's busiest seafronts. Plus, a couple of expats attempt make a success of their huge holiday complex in the middle of one of Europe's only desert regions.(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Presenter Tim Shaw and master mechanic Fuzz Townshend lead a team of experts who secretly restore classic cars for their unsuspecting owners. They begin by coming to the aid of a bricklayer whose 1986 Ford Sierra RS Cosworth was neglected after a family tragedy.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: A recent storm leaves Lee and Belinda's castle keep in need of urgent repairs - and Lee must face his fear of heights if he is to repair the hole in the roof.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows the progress of Stephen Yeoman and Anita Findlay, who want to build a cutting-edge, post-industrial house covered in rusty metal. However, their prominent riverside plot in the traditional and architecturally conservative area of South Downs means everyone will be watching. The project proves to be a real rollercoaster when the fitting of the rusty steel cladding goes wrong, cash flow problems threaten to bring work to a halt, and the couple announce that a baby is on the way.(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw take on a 1983 Citroen 2CV after its owner was unable to continue its restoration after being diagnosed with stage 4 throat cancer.(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Sean Lock and Victoria Coren Mitchell take on Jon Richardson and James Acaster, while Morgana Robinson joins wordsmith Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Hosted by Jimmy Carr, with regular Countdown mathematician Rachel Riley looking after the numbers and letters.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Best Loved Sitcoms
programme.desc: Friday Night Dinner star Tamsin Greig narrates the results of a nationwide poll seeking to identify the nation's most revered sitcom, featuring contributions from Brendan O'Carroll, Richard Wilson, Tony Robinson, Ricky Tomlinson, Wendy Craig, Patricia Routledge, Leslie Ash and Alison Steadman.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The A&E documentary series turns its attention to caring and kindness at St George's Hospital in south-west London, highlighting altruistic acts from staff, patients and visitors alike. A 13-year-old cyclist experiencing bouts of unconsciousness is sent for a CT scan, and 84-year-old Ursula, who was born in Germany, arrives in resus having lost a lot of blood during a series of severe nose-bleeds. Plus, 76-year-old Margaret is brought in to St George's after suffering a suspected stroke during a routine appointment at another hospital, and the doctors must quickly administer a clot-busting drug if they are to prevent further damage to her brain.(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Sean Lock and Victoria Coren Mitchell take on Jon Richardson and James Acaster, while Morgana Robinson joins wordsmith Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Hosted by Jimmy Carr, with regular Countdown mathematician Rachel Riley looking after the numbers and letters.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Best Loved Sitcoms
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating some of the many classic sitcoms that have entertained British audiences over the past 50 years, narrated by Tamsin Greig, herself a star of the likes of Friday Night Dinner and Black Books. Along the way, she takes in everything from modern 'game-changers' such as The Office, Twenty Twelve and Peep Show, to vintage shows including The Likely Lads, Dad's Army and Steptoe and Son. Making use of a cross-generational poll, she presents a countdown that reveals which show has become the most revered sitcom of all, and a host of stars including Mrs Brown's Boys' Brendan O'Carroll, One Foot in the Grave's Richard Wilson, Blackadder's Tony Robinson and Keeping Up Appearances' Patricia Routledge share their thoughts on working on some of the nation's most treasured comedy dramas.(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty visits Europe's biggest brewery - Heineken in the Netherlands - on a mission to explain the difference between lager and ale. His journey takes a surprising twist when he comes face to face with the mother of all lagers. Kate Quilton sets Matt Tebbutt the challenge of making crumpets, and finds out how they get filled with unique holes at the Warburtons factory in Enfield, north London. Matt also heads for snowy Canada to discover why maple syrup is so expensive, and finds out that maple syrup robberies are a big concern to the industry.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp enters the shortbread competition at the Chagford Show in Devon, as she tries to woo the judges with some creative takes on the classic treat, including using rosemary and lavender as ingredients.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: London couple Bob and Annette Roberts have been holidaying in Grenada for 25 years and now, with retirement on the horizon, they're looking to invest Â£225,000 in a holiday home. Adrian Simpson shows them round a selection of what is available on the market.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Melinda Beckett-Hughes is the final host of the week at Ayuda House in Greater Manchester - and as she has spent the week picking fault, the guests expect perfection from her. However, Robert and Sylvia are shocked to find a dirty towel in a drawer, while Emmanuel and Eileen cannot believe the levels of dust and Connie is horrified by the carpet.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and settle some old scores. Eileen and Emmanuel from North Bridge Street want to get to the bottom of Melinda and Stewart's dirty bin comments as well as challenging Melinda's shower issues. Robert and Sylvia from the Golden Fleece try to defend their breakfast disaster, but also pull Eileen and Emmanuel up on their low score.(n)

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: A couple welcome guests to their newly opened holiday complex in the Spanish desert, but a leak in the pool threatens to scupper their big plans. In France, a family renovating a gite wonder if they've bitten off more than they can chew and two new bar-owners in Spain welcome the first tourists of the season.(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Presenter Tim Shaw and master mechanic Fuzz Townshend lead a team of experts as they attempt to renovate a Volkswagen Beetle that has spent many years rusting in a garage.(n)

programme.desc: Stephanie enlists close family friend Gerry and mother Isobel to get her B&B room finished in time, and Dick offers his advice to a London couple constructing a water feature.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud revisits Andy and Nicky Bruce, who were building an experimental amphibious house on a small island on the Thames in Buckinghamshire. They spent Â£1.2million on one of the most ambitious Grand Designs ever, but the logistical headache of building on an island only accessible by a narrow footbridge soon became clear. Heavy machinery was swept away by the river, contractors were changed, and the site was hit by severe storms, causing delays and resulting in an unfinished and untested building. Kevin returns to find out what living on a regularly flooded piece of land is like, and whether the floating home performed when the river burst its banks in January 2015.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A look at the night shift in a busy A&E department. A woman with a severe form of autism is brought in, having suffered an injury to one of her fingers. While she is treated, her sister shares stories of their childhood and the challenges their family have faced. An 82-year-old patient has difficulty breathing, and doctors suspect he has developed life-threatening sepsis and need to act fast to try to halt the spread of infection.(n)

programme.desc: Cameras follow the paramedic teams of West Midlands Ambulance Service, beginning with a crew treating a man found collapsed in his garden in Birmingham.(n)

programme.desc: Searching for some tech to fix Starbug's faulty steering, Lister and his crew dock at a research station where scientists claim to have manufactured a 'cure' for evil. Sci-fi comedy, starring Craig Charles, Danny-John Jules and Chris Barrie.(n)

Steven Spielberg's fantasy adventure, with Robin Williams, Dustin Hoffman, Julia Roberts, Bob Hoskins and Maggie Smith.(n)

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: The presenter examines how the car industry in Germany and Japan blossomed following the Second World War, becoming manufacturing giants that far outstripped the Allied nations that had defeated them. He test drives some of the vehicles Germany and Japan produced during the post-war period, as well as an assortment of other classic cars, including E-Types and Mustangs. He also reveals the sad tale of the Austin Allegro, a vehicle that had a devastating impact on the British car industry.(n)

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James charts the history of 4x4 vehicles from the jeeps of the Second World war onwards, revealing how they rose in popularity in everyday use and in rallies. He explores the rivalry between Land Rover and Land Cruiser, and puts some classic vehicles through their paces by driving up Mount Fuji and through the Mojave desert.(n)

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James looks at some of the advances in motoring that never caught on, revealing how the petrol engine came to dominate in the 20th century, while concepts for jet engines and steam power fell by the wayside. He also drives an amphibious car and an electric scooter, and takes a ride in the world's fastest driverless car.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The team sets off on a high-altitude trek from Kathmandu, capital of Nepal, to the Forbidden City of Lo Manthang, a secretive kingdom perched high on the Tibetan plateau, on the far side of the Himalayas. On a five-day expedition through the highest mountains, deepest gorges and harshest terrain on the planet, Paddy McGuinness, Freddie Flintoff and Chris Harris battle huge river crossings, vast mudslides and dizzying mountain passes on a trip that would test even the hardiest new 4x4s to the limit. But Paddy, Freddie and Chris aren't crossing the Himalayas in new 4x4s. They're crossing the Himalayas in a small old Peugeot, a very compact old Renault, and Nepal's first (and only) home-built car.(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Actor Mackenzie Crook, wildlife presenter Chris Packham, writer and broadcaster Victoria Coren Mitchell and comedian Rhod Gilbert join David Mitchell and Lee Mack for the comedy panel show. Rob Brydon chairs as the contestants try to hoodwink their opponents with odd personal statements that may or may not be true.(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Lorraine Kelly and Dara O Briain join David Mitchell's team while Barry Cryer and Sue Perkins are captained by Lee Mack, on the panel game where contestants try to fool their opponents into mistaking fact for fiction and vice versa. Hosted by Rob Brydon.(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Extended edition of Sandi Toksvig's first episode as host of the panel game, in which she invites Alan Davies, Cariad Lloyd, Romesh Ranganathan and Phill Jupitus to answer questions on subjects associated with the letter n. programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May put three supercars through their paces in Italy. Their weapons of choice are the 691bhp Lamborghini Aventador, McLaren's hi-tech MP4-12C, and a British brute in the form of the Noble M600. The journey begins at Nardo, a high-speed testing ground so vast it can be seen from space, and includes a nerve-racking drive in Rome before reaching its conclusion with a timed challenge against the Stig at the Imola circuit.(n)

programme.title: Red Bull Soapbox Race
programme.desc: The amateur race arrives into Valkenburg aan de Geul in the Netherlands, traditionally the finish of the Amstel Gold Classic cycling race.(n)

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: The presenter examines how the car industry in Germany and Japan blossomed following the Second World War, becoming manufacturing giants that far outstripped the Allied nations that had defeated them. He test drives some of the vehicles Germany and Japan produced during the post-war period, as well as an assortment of other classic cars, including E-Types and Mustangs. He also reveals the sad tale of the Austin Allegro, a vehicle that had a devastating impact on the British car industry.(n)

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James charts the history of 4x4 vehicles from the jeeps of the Second World war onwards, revealing how they rose in popularity in everyday use and in rallies. He explores the rivalry between Land Rover and Land Cruiser, and puts some classic vehicles through their paces by driving up Mount Fuji and through the Mojave desert.(n)

programme.desc: Stephen Fry hosts another round of the peculiar panel quiz and asks a range of unusual questions about jobs, finding out how much Sarah Millican, David Mitchell, the Rev Richard Coles and regular panellist Alan Davies know about the subject.(n)

Host Rob Brydon oversees proceedings as the contestants try to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves on the comedy panel show.(n)

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies returns to set tasks for Dave Gorman, Al Murray, Rob Beckett, Sara Pascoe and Paul Chowdhry involving vegetable propulsion, snowmen and Swedes.(n)

programme.title: Dave Gorman Goodish Hits
programme.desc: The comedian presents highlights from all three series of Dave Gorman's Modern Life Is Goodish, as well as some scenes which have never been seen on British TV.(n)

programme.title: Operation Gold Rush with Dan Snow
programme.desc: The historian follows in the footsteps of 19th-century gold prospectors, attempting to find riches in a remote region on the edge of the Canadian arctic. He is joined on his expedition by polar explorer Felicity Aston and physiologist Dr Kevin Fong. The first challenge of the trek is to cross Alaska's Coast Mountains, where they retrace two routes set by 19th-century miners and brave icy rivers, avalanche-prone slopes and near vertical ascents.(n)

programme.title: Operation Gold Rush with Dan Snow
programme.desc: Dan Snow, Felicity Aston and Dr Kevin Fong build a wooden boat, following original designs for the vessels used during the Gold Rush. This vessel must carry them across hundreds of miles of treacherous lakes and rivers, including the infamous One Mile Rapids, a narrow canyon of white water and jagged rocks.(n)

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: Henry Ford's plan, with the help of Albert Kahn, to build a giant factory in Michigan, covering 3.5million sq ft and including an aircraft production line half a mile in length.(n)

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: The sabotaging efforts of Peugeot in Nazi-occupied France, where the bosses and workers cooperated closely with the Resistance to hinder the use of military vehicles by the Nazis.(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: Predators and prey in the open oceans come under the scrutiny of David Attenborough. Marlin track down a shoal of sardines as a sei whale scoops up the leftovers, dolphins herd mackerel and attract the attention of a sailfish with the resonance of their sonar, and giant sunfish queue up to allow other species, including seagulls, to feed on their parasites.(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: Marine life at the poles, where winter temperatures frequently fall to a numbing -40C. Even when the sun reappears in the spring, its weak rays take a long time to melt the ice. David Attenborough describes how wildlife including seals, whales, polar bears and penguins overcome the elements to survive through prolonged cold spells.(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: An exploration of the world's most densely inhabited waters, where variations in temperature owing to the year-long cycle of the sun encourage a vast array of marine life, from seals and sharks in the winter, to lobster and salmon in the summer, dolphins in the autumn and the walking handfish in spring. Narrated by David Attenborough.(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: Veteran naturalist David Attenborough explores the fragile marine ecosystem of the coral reef, where all creatures, including the corals themselves, have to fight for survival. From tiny beginnings, the reef grows and develops over hundreds of years, providing support for innumerable animals - yet one storm can threaten the entire community.(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough investigates the opportunities tides create for marine life, showing giant stingrays gliding on currents for a rest, South African sea snails surfing the waves in pursuit of a meal, crabs playing football on a beach, and bottlenose dolphins embarking on a dangerous game of catch. In the oceanic world each turn of the tide can mean life or death for its inhabitants.(n)

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: Exploring the wealth of marine life that inhabits the ocean's edge, dealing with the dangers of an ever-shifting environment. David Attenborough observes nesting seabirds in east Russia, Patagonian killer whales, Newfoundland caplin fish, walruses, and Brazilian green turtles - which make their annual expedition to an island in the middle of the Atlantic to lay their eggs.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of Britain
programme.desc: The documentary uncovering the hidden history of Britain continues with the story of the department store Selfridges, looking at the ways it changed the country's shopping habits. Plus, an examination of the life of Mr Selfridge himself.(n)

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: British troops save Edith and Rene from execution, and the pair are released into the woods. Meanwhile, the Germans decide to head for Spain, and Michelle joins Crabtree in a duet. Classic wartime sitcom, starring Gorden Kaye and Carmen Silvera.(n)

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: The Colonel and Gruber's escape plan has gone awry, so they try to make general Von Klinkerhoffen believe the Resistance had kidnapped them.(n)

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Rene is imprisoned by communist leader Louise, who plans to use him as her love slave, while Mimi, Edith and Yvette are convinced he is dead and decide to hold a seance.(n)

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Four VW camper vans pitch up at the auction house looking for a new home and Derek picks up an Austin 16 that 'goes like stink and has some unusual modern modifications'.(n)

programme.title: History Hunters
programme.desc: A former dealer is selling his entire medal collection - will Dave and Steve find anything worth bidding for? And Steve digs deep into a WW1 army medic's family history.(n)

programme.title: Warbird Workshop
programme.desc: At St Athan in Wales, engineer John Sparks and his team of ex-Navy and RAF engineers begin the task of reassembling the Czech-built jet from nine parts delivered by lorry.(n)
